chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `LPE` field in `henvcfg`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When the `LPE` field is set to 1, the Zicfilp extension is enabled in VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When the `LPE` field is 0, the Zicfilp extension is not enabled in VS-mode and the following rules apply to VS-mode:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: The hart does not update the `ELP` state; it remains as `NOLPEXPECTED`. * The `LPAD` instruction operates as a no-op.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: The Zicfiss extension adds the `SSE` field in `henvcfg`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: If the `SSE` field is set to 1, the Zicfiss extension is activated in VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When the `SSE` field is 0, the Zicfiss extension remains inactive in VS-mode, and the following rules apply when `V=1`:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: 32-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zimop. * 16-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zcmop. * The `pte.xwr=010b` encoding in VS-stage page tables becomes reserved. * The `senvcfg.SSE` field will read as zero and is read-only. *
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When `menvcfg.SSE` is one, `SSAMOSWAP.W/D` raises a virtual-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: The Ssdbltrp extension adds the double-trap-enable (`DTE`) field in `henvcfg`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When `henvcfg.DTE` is zero, the implementation behaves as though Ssdbltrp is not implemented for VS-mode and the `vsstatus.SDT` bit is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: When XLEN=32, `henvcfgh` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `henvcfg`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: Register `henvcfgh` does not exist when XLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Environment Configuration Register (`henvcfg`)

chunks.text: The counter-enable register `hcounteren` is a 32-bit register that controls the availability of the hardware performance monitoring counters to the guest virtual machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: When the CY, TM, IR, or HPMn bit in the `hcounteren` register is clear, attempts to read the `cycle`, `time`, `instret`, or `hpmcounter` n register while V=1 will cause a virtual-instruction exception if the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: When one of these bits is set, access to the corresponding register is permitted when V=1, unless prevented for some other reason.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: In VU-mode, a counter is not readable unless the applicable bits are set in both `hcounteren` and `scounteren`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: In addition, when the TM bit in the `hcounteren` register is clear, attempts to access the `vstimecmp` register (via `stimecmp`) while executing in VS-mode will cause a virtual-instruction exception if the same bit in `mcounteren` is set.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: When this bit and the same bit in `mcounteren` are both set, access to the `vstimecmp` register (if implemented) is permitted in VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: `hcounteren` must be implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: However, any of the bits may be read-only zero, indicating reads to the corresponding counter will cause an exception when V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: Hence, they are effectively *WARL* fields.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Counter-Enable (`hcounteren`) Register

chunks.text: The `htimedelta` CSR is a 64-bit read/write register that contains the delta between the value of the `time` CSR and the value returned in VS-mode or VU-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

chunks.text: That is, reading the `time` CSR in VS or VU mode returns the sum of the contents of `htimedelta` and the actual value of `time`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

chunks.text: When XLEN=32, `htimedeltah` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `htimedelta`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

chunks.text: Register `htimedeltah` does not exist when XLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

chunks.text: If the `time` CSR is implemented, `htimedelta` (and `htimedeltah` for XLEN=32) must be implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Time Delta (`htimedelta`) Register

chunks.text: The `htval` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into HS-mode, `htval` is written with additional exception-specific information, alongside `stval`, to assist software in handling the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: When a guest-page-fault trap is taken into HS-mode, `htval` is written with either zero or the guest physical address that faulted, shifted right by 2 bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: For other traps, `htval` is set to zero, but a future standard or extension may redefine `htval's` setting for other traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: Otherwise, for misaligned loads and stores that cause guest-page faults, a nonzero guest physical address in `htval` corresponds to the faulting portion of the access as indicated by the virtual address in `stval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: For instruction guest-page faults on systems with variable-length instructions, a nonzero `htval` corresponds to the faulting portion of the instruction as indicated by the virtual address in `stval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: `htval` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold zero and may be capable of holding only an arbitrary subset of other 2-bit-shifted guest physical addresses, if any.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Value (`htval`) Register

chunks.text: The `htinst` register is an HSXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into HS-mode, `htinst` is written with a value that, if nonzero, provides information about the instruction that trapped, to assist software in handling the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

chunks.text: The values that may be written to `htinst` on a trap are documented in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

chunks.text: `htinst` is a *WARL* register that need only be able to hold the values that the implementation may automatically write to it on a trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Trap Instruction (`htinst`) Register

chunks.text: Similar to CSR `satp`, this register holds the physical page number (PPN) of the guest-physical root page table; a virtual machine identifier (VMID), which facilitates address-translation fences on a per-virtual-machine basis; and the MODE field, which selects the address-translation scheme for guest physical addresses.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: When `mstatus`.TVM=1, attempts to read or write `hgatp` while executing in HS-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: shows the encodings of the MODE field when HSXLEN=32 and HSXLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: When MODE=Bare, guest physical addresses are equal to supervisor physical addresses, and there is no further memory protection for a guest virtual machine beyond the physical memory protection scheme described in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: In this case, software must write zero to the remaining fields in `hgatp`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: Attempting to select MODE=Bare with a nonzero pattern in the remaining fields has an UNSPECIFIED effect on the value that the remaining fields assume and an UNSPECIFIED effect on G-stage address translation and protection behavior.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: When HSXLEN=32, the only other valid setting for MODE is Sv32x4, which is a modification of the usual Sv32 paged virtual-memory scheme, extended to support 34-bit guest physical addresses.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: When HSXLEN=64, modes Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4 are defined as modifications of the Sv39, Sv48, and Sv57 paged virtual-memory schemes.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: All of these paged virtual-memory schemes are described in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The remaining MODE settings when HSXLEN=64 are reserved for future use and may define different interpretations of the other fields in `hgatp`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: Implementations are not required to support all defined MODE settings when HSXLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: A write to `hgatp` with an unsupported MODE value is not ignored as it is for `satp`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: Instead, the fields of `hgatp` are *WARL* in the normal way, when so indicated.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: As explained in , for the paged virtual-memory schemes (Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4), the root page table is 16 KiB and must be aligned to a 16-KiB boundary.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: In these modes, the lowest two bits of the physical page number (PPN) in `hgatp` always read as zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: An implementation that supports only the defined paged virtual-memory schemes and/or Bare may make PPN[1:0] read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The number of VMID bits is UNSPECIFIED and may be zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The number of implemented VMID bits, termed VMIDLEN, may be determined by writing one to every bit position in the VMID field, then reading back the value in `hgatp` to see which bit positions in the VMID field hold a one.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The maximal value of VMIDLEN, termed VMIDMAX, is 7 for Sv32x4 or 14 for Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The `hgatp` register is considered active for the purposes of the address-translation algorithm unless the effective privilege mode is U and `hstatus`.HU=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Hypervisor Guest Address Translation and Protection (`hgatp`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsstatus` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `sstatus`, formatted as shown in when VSXLEN=32 and when VSXLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vsstatus` substitutes for the usual `sstatus`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sstatus` actually access `vsstatus` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: The UXL field controls the effective XLEN for VU-mode, which may differ from the XLEN for VS-mode (VSXLEN).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: When VSXLEN=32, the UXL field does not exist, and VU-mode XLEN=32.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: When VSXLEN=64, UXL is a *WARL* field that is encoded the same as the MXL field of `misa`, shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: In particular, an implementation may make UXL be a read-only copy of field VSXL of `hstatus`, forcing VU-mode XLEN=VSXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: If VSXLEN is changed from 32 to a wider width, and if field UXL is not restricted to a single value, it gets the value corresponding to the widest supported width not wider than the new VSXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, both `vsstatus`.FS and the HS-level `sstatus`.FS are in effect.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Attempts to execute a floating-point instruction when either field is 0 (Off) raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Modifying the floating-point state when V=1 causes both fields to be set to 3 (Dirty).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Similarly, when V=1, both `vsstatus`.VS and the HS-level `sstatus`.VS are in effect.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Attempts to execute a vector instruction when either field is 0 (Off) raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Modifying the vector state when V=1 causes both fields to be set to 3 (Dirty).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: Read-only fields SD and XS summarize the extension context status as it is visible to VS-mode only.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: An implementation may make field UBE be a read-only copy of `hstatus`.VSBE.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vsstatus` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine, unless a virtual-machine load/store (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) or the MPRV feature in the `mstatus` register is used to execute a load or store as though V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `SPELP` field that holds the previous `ELP`, and is updated as specified in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: The `SPELP` field is encoded as follows:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: 0 - `NOLPEXPECTED` - no landing pad instruction expected. * 1 - `LPEXPECTED` - a landing pad instruction is expected.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Status (`vsstatus`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsip` and `vsie` registers are VSXLEN-bit read/write registers that are VS-mode’s versions of supervisor CSRs `sip` and `sie`, formatted as shown in and respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: When V=1, `vsip` and `vsie` substitute for the usual `sip` and `sie`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sip`/`sie` actually access `vsip`/`vsie` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: However, interrupts directed to HS-level continue to be indicated in the HS-level `sip` register, not in `vsip`, when V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: The standard portions (bits 15:0) of registers `vsip` and `vsie` are formatted as shown in and respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: Extension Shlcofideleg supports delegating LCOFI interrupts to VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: If the Shlcofideleg extension is implemented, `hideleg` bit 13 is writable; otherwise, it is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: When bit 13 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.LCOFIP and `vsie`.LCOFIE are read-only zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: Else, `vsip`.LCOFIP and `vsie`.LCOFIE are aliases of `sip`.LCOFIP and `sie`.LCOFIE.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: When bit 10 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.SEIP and `vsie`.SEIE are read-only zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: Else, `vsip`.SEIP and `vsie`.SEIE are aliases of `hip`.VSEIP and `hie`.VSEIE.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: When bit 6 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.STIP and `vsie`.STIE are read-only zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: Else, `vsip`.STIP and `vsie`.STIE are aliases of `hip`.VSTIP and `hie`.VSTIE.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: When bit 2 of `hideleg` is zero, `vsip`.SSIP and `vsie`.SSIE are read-only zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: Else, `vsip`.SSIP and `vsie`.SSIE are aliases of `hip`.VSSIP and `hie`.VSSIE.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Interrupt (`vsip` and `vsie`) Registers

chunks.text: The `vstvec` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `stvec`, formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`vstvec`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vstvec` substitutes for the usual `stvec`, so instructions that normally read or modify `stvec` actually access `vstvec` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`vstvec`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vstvec` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Vector Base Address (`vstvec`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsscratch` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `sscratch`, formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Scratch (`vsscratch`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vsscratch` substitutes for the usual `sscratch`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sscratch` actually access `vsscratch` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Scratch (`vsscratch`) Register

chunks.text: The contents of `vsscratch` never directly affect the behavior of the machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Scratch (`vsscratch`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsepc` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `sepc`, formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`vsepc`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vsepc` substitutes for the usual `sepc`, so instructions that normally read or modify `sepc` actually access `vsepc` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`vsepc`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vsepc` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`vsepc`) Register

chunks.text: `vsepc` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold the same set of values that `sepc` can hold.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Exception Program Counter (`vsepc`) Register

chunks.text: The `vscause` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `scause`, formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Cause (`vscause`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vscause` substitutes for the usual `scause`, so instructions that normally read or modify `scause` actually access `vscause` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Cause (`vscause`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vscause` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Cause (`vscause`) Register

chunks.text: `vscause` is a *WLRL* register that must be able to hold the same set of values that `scause` can hold.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Cause (`vscause`) Register

chunks.text: The `vstval` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `stval`, formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Value (`vstval`) Register

chunks.text: When V=1, `vstval` substitutes for the usual `stval`, so instructions that normally read or modify `stval` actually access `vstval` instead.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Value (`vstval`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vstval` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Value (`vstval`) Register

chunks.text: `vstval` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold the same set of values that `stval` can hold.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Trap Value (`vstval`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsatp` register is a VSXLEN-bit read/write register that is VS-mode’s version of supervisor register `satp`, formatted as shown in for VSXLEN=32 and for VSXLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: The `vsatp` register is considered active for the purposes of the address-translation algorithm unless the effective privilege mode is U and `hstatus`.HU=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: However, even when `vsatp` is active, VS-stage page-table entries’ A bits must not be set as a result of speculative execution, unless the effective privilege mode is VS or VU.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, a write to `vsatp` with an unsupported MODE value is either ignored as it is for `satp`, or the fields of `vsatp` are treated as *WARL* in the normal way.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: However, when V=1, a write to `satp` with an unsupported MODE value is ignored and no write to `vsatp` is effected.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: When V=0, `vsatp` does not directly affect the behavior of the machine, unless a virtual-machine load/store (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) or the MPRV feature in the `mstatus` register is used to execute a load or store as though V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (`vsatp`) Register

chunks.text: The `vstimecmp` CSR is a 64-bit register and has 64-bit precision on all RV32 and RV64 systems.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

chunks.text: In RV32 only, accesses to the `vstimecmp` CSR access the low 32 bits, while accesses to the `vstimecmph` CSR access the high 32 bits of `vstimecmp`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

chunks.text: If the result of this comparison changes, it is guaranteed to be reflected in VSTIP eventually, but not necessarily immediately.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

chunks.text: The interrupt will be taken based on the standard interrupt enable and delegation rules while V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor and Virtual Supervisor CSRs > Virtual Supervisor Timer (`vstimecmp`) Register

chunks.text: The hypervisor virtual-machine load and store instructions are valid only in M-mode or HS-mode, or in U-mode when `hstatus`.HU=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: The effective privilege mode of the explicit memory access is VU when `hstatus`.SPVP=0, and VS when `hstatus`.SPVP=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: As usual for VS-mode and VU-mode, two-stage address translation is applied, and the HS-level `sstatus`.SUM is ignored. HS-level `sstatus`.MXR makes execute-only pages readable by explicit loads for both stages of address translation (VS-stage and G-stage), whereas `vsstatus`.MXR affects only the first translation stage (VS-stage).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: That is, the memory being read must be executable in both stages of address translation, but read permission is not required.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: For the supervisor physical address that results from address translation, the supervisor physical memory attributes must grant both execute and read permissions. (The supervisor physical memory attributes are the machine’s physical memory attributes as modified by physical memory protection, , for supervisor level.)
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: HLVX.WU is valid for RV32, even though LWU and HLV.WU are not. (For RV32, HLVX.WU can be considered a variant of HLV.W, as sign extension is irrelevant for 32-bit values.)
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: Attempts to execute a virtual-machine load/store instruction (HLV, HLVX, or HSV) when V=1 cause a virtual-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: Attempts to execute one of these same instructions from U-mode when `hstatus`.HU=0 cause an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions

chunks.text: The hypervisor memory-management fence instructions, HFENCE.VVMA and HFENCE.GVMA, perform a function similar to SFENCE.VMA (), except applying to the VS-level memory-management data structures controlled by CSR `vsatp` (HFENCE.VVMA) or the guest-physical memory-management data structures controlled by CSR `hgatp` (HFENCE.GVMA).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Instruction SFENCE.VMA applies only to the memory-management data structures controlled by the current `satp` (either the HS-level `satp` when V=0 or `vsatp` when V=1).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: are subsequent to the HFENCE.VVMA, and * execute when `hgatp`.VMID has the same setting as it did when HFENCE.VVMA executed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Implicit reads need not be ordered when `hgatp`.VMID is different than at the time HFENCE.VVMA executed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: If operand rs1!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest virtual address, and if operand rs2!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest address-space identifier (ASID).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: When rs2!=`x0`, bits XLEN-1:ASIDMAX of the value held in rs2 are reserved for future standard use.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Until their use is defined by a standard extension, they should be zeroed by software and ignored by current implementations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Furthermore, if ASIDLEN < ASIDMAX, the implementation shall ignore bits ASIDMAX-1:ASIDLEN of the value held in rs2.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Neither `mstatus`.TVM nor `hstatus`.VTVM causes HFENCE.VVMA to trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: HFENCE.GVMA is valid only in HS-mode when `mstatus`.TVM=0, or in M-mode (irrespective of `mstatus`.TVM).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: If operand rs1!=`x0`, it specifies a single guest physical address, shifted right by 2 bits, and if operand rs2!=`x0`, it specifies a single virtual machine identifier (VMID).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: When rs2!=`x0`, bits XLEN-1:VMIDMAX of the value held in rs2 are reserved for future standard use.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Until their use is defined by a standard extension, they should be zeroed by software and ignored by current implementations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Furthermore, if VMIDLEN < VMIDMAX, the implementation shall ignore bits VMIDMAX-1:VMIDLEN of the value held in rs2.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: If `hgatp`.MODE is changed for a given VMID, an HFENCE.GVMA with rs1=`x0` (and rs2 set to either `x0` or the VMID) must be executed to order subsequent guest translations with the MODE change—even if the old MODE or new MODE is Bare.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Attempts to execute HFENCE.VVMA or HFENCE.GVMA when V=1 cause a virtual-instruction exception, while attempts to do the same in U-mode cause an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: Attempting to execute HFENCE.GVMA in HS-mode when `mstatus`.TVM=1 also causes an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Hypervisor Instructions > Hypervisor Memory-Management Fence Instructions

chunks.text: The hypervisor extension augments or modifies machine CSRs `mstatus`, `mstatush`, `mideleg`, `mip`, and `mie`, and adds CSRs `mtval2` and `mtinst`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs

chunks.text: The hypervisor extension adds two fields, MPV and GVA, to the machine-level `mstatus` or `mstatush` CSR, and modifies the behavior of several existing `mstatus` fields. shows the modified `mstatus` register when the hypervisor extension is implemented and MXLEN=64.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: When MXLEN=32, the hypervisor extension adds MPV and GVA not to `mstatus` but to `mstatush`. shows the `mstatush` register when the hypervisor extension is implemented and MXLEN=32.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The format of `mstatus` is unchanged for RV32. include::images/bytefield/hypv-mstatush.edn[]
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The MPV bit (Machine Previous Virtualization Mode) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into M-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Just as the MPP field is set to the (nominal) privilege mode at the time of the trap, the MPV bit is set to the value of the virtualization mode V at the time of the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: When an MRET instruction is executed, the virtualization mode V is set to MPV, unless MPP=3, in which case V remains 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Field GVA (Guest Virtual Address) is written by the implementation whenever a trap is taken into M-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: For any trap (breakpoint, address misaligned, access fault, page fault, or guest-page fault) that writes a guest virtual address to `mtval`, GVA is set to 1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: For any other trap into M-mode, GVA is set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The TSR and TVM fields of `mstatus` affect execution only in HS-mode, not in VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The TW field affects execution in all modes except M-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Setting TVM=1 prevents HS-mode from accessing `hgatp` or executing HFENCE.GVMA or HINVAL.GVMA, but has no effect on accesses to `vsatp` or instructions HFENCE.VVMA or HINVAL.VVMA.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The hypervisor extension changes the behavior of the Modify Privilege field, MPRV, of `mstatus`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: When MPRV=0, translation and protection behave as normal.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: When MPRV=1, explicit memory accesses are translated and protected, and endianness is applied, as though the current virtualization mode were set to MPV and the current nominal privilege mode were set to MPP. enumerates the cases.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: |1 |0 |0 |U-level access with HS-level translation and protection only.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: |1 |0 |1 |HS-level access with HS-level translation and protection only.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: |1 |- |3 |M-level access with no translation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: |1 |1 |0 |VU-level access with two-stage translation and protection.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The HS-level MXR bit makes any executable page readable. `vsstatus`.MXR makes readable those pages marked executable at the VS translation stage, but only if readable at the guest-physical translation stage.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: |1 |1 |1 |VS-level access with two-stage translation and protection.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The HS-level MXR bit makes any executable page readable. `vsstatus`.MXR makes readable those pages marked executable at the VS translation stage, but only if readable at the guest-physical translation stage. `vsstatus`.SUM applies instead of the HS-level SUM bit. |
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: MPRV does not affect the virtual-machine load/store instructions, HLV, HLVX, and HSV.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The explicit loads and stores of these instructions always act as though V=1 and the nominal privilege mode were `hstatus`.SPVP, overriding MPRV.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The `mstatus` register is a superset of the HS-level `sstatus` register but is not a superset of `vsstatus`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: When the hypervisor extension is implemented, bits 10, 6, and 2 of `mideleg` (corresponding to the standard VS-level interrupts) are each read-only one.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

chunks.text: Furthermore, if any guest external interrupts are implemented (GEILEN is nonzero), bit 12 of `mideleg` (corresponding to supervisor-level guest external interrupts) is also read-only one. VS-level interrupts and guest external interrupts are always delegated past M-mode to HS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

chunks.text: For bits of `mideleg` that are zero, the corresponding bits in `hideleg`, `hip`, and `hie` are read-only zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register

chunks.text: The hypervisor extension gives registers `mip` and `mie` additional active bits for the hypervisor-added interrupts. and show the standard portions (bits 15:0) of registers `mip` and `mie` when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

chunks.text: Bits SGEIP, VSEIP, VSTIP, and VSSIP in `mip` are aliases for the same bits in hypervisor CSR `hip`, while SGEIE, VSEIE, VSTIE, and VSSIE in `mie` are aliases for the same bits in `hie`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

chunks.text: The `mtval2` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mtval2` is written with additional exception-specific information, alongside `mtval`, to assist software in handling the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: When a guest-page-fault trap is taken into M-mode, `mtval2` is written with either zero or the guest physical address that faulted, shifted right by 2 bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: For other traps, `mtval2` is set to zero, but a future standard or extension may redefine `mtval2's` setting for other traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: If a guest-page fault is due to an implicit memory access during first-stage (VS-stage) address translation, a guest physical address written to `mtval2` is that of the implicit memory access that faulted.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: Additional information is provided in CSR `mtinst` to disambiguate such situations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: Otherwise, for misaligned loads and stores that cause guest-page faults, a nonzero guest physical address in `mtval2` corresponds to the faulting portion of the access as indicated by the virtual address in `mtval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: For instruction guest-page faults on systems with variable-length instructions, a nonzero `mtval2` corresponds to the faulting portion of the instruction as indicated by the virtual address in `mtval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: `mtval2` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold zero and may be capable of holding only an arbitrary subset of other 2-bit-shifted guest physical addresses, if any.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Second Trap Value (`mtval2`) Register

chunks.text: The `mtinst` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mtinst` is written with a value that, if nonzero, provides information about the instruction that trapped, to assist software in handling the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

chunks.text: The values that may be written to `mtinst` on a trap are documented in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

chunks.text: `mtinst` is a *WARL* register that need only be able to hold the values that the implementation may automatically write to it on a trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Instruction (`mtinst`) Register

chunks.text: For any virtual memory access, the original virtual address is converted in the first stage by VS-level address translation, as controlled by the `vsatp` register, into a guest physical address.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

chunks.text: The guest physical address is then converted in the second stage by guest physical address translation, as controlled by the `hgatp` register, into a supervisor physical address.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

chunks.text: Although there is no option to disable two-stage address translation when V=1, either stage of translation can be effectively disabled by zeroing the corresponding `vsatp` or `hgatp` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

chunks.text: The `vsstatus` field MXR, which makes execute-only pages readable by explicit loads, only overrides VS-stage page protection.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

chunks.text: When V=1, memory accesses that would normally bypass address translation are subject to G-stage address translation alone.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation

chunks.text: The mapping of guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses is controlled by CSR `hgatp` ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: When the address translation scheme selected by the MODE field of `hgatp` is Bare, guest physical addresses are equal to supervisor physical addresses without modification, and no memory protection applies in the trivial translation of guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: When `hgatp`.MODE specifies a translation scheme of Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, or Sv57x4, G-stage address translation is a variation on the usual page-based virtual address translation scheme of Sv32, Sv39, Sv48, or Sv57, respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: In each case, the size of the incoming address is widened by 2 bits (to 34, 41, 50, or 59 bits).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Matching its larger size, the root page table also must be aligned to a 16 KiB boundary instead of the usual 4 KiB page boundary.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Address bits 63:41 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Address bits 63:50 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Address bits 63:59 must all be zeros, or else a guest-page-fault exception occurs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: `hgatp` substitutes for the usual `satp`; * for the translation to begin, the effective privilege mode must be VS-mode or VU-mode; * when checking the U bit, the current privilege mode is always taken to be U-mode; and * guest-page-fault exceptions are raised instead of regular page-fault exceptions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Access type permissions—readable, writable, or executable—are checked during G-stage translation the same as for VS-stage translation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: For a memory access made to support VS-stage address translation (such as to read/write a VS-level page table), permissions and the need to set A and/or D bits at the G-stage level are checked as though for an implicit load or store, not for the original access type.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: However, any exception is always reported for the original access type (instruction, load, or store/AMO).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Until its use is defined by a standard extension, it should be cleared by software for forward compatibility, and must be ignored by hardware.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest Physical Address Translation

chunks.text: Guest-page-fault traps may be delegated from M-mode to HS-mode under the control of CSR `medeleg`, but cannot be delegated to other privilege modes.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: On a guest-page fault, CSR `mtval` or `stval` is written with the faulting guest virtual address as usual, and `mtval2` or `htval` is written either with zero or with the faulting guest physical address, shifted right by 2 bits. CSR `mtinst` or `htinst` may also be written with information about the faulting instruction or other reason for the access, as explained in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: When an instruction fetch or a misaligned memory access straddles a page boundary, two different address translations are involved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: When a guest-page fault occurs in such a circumstance, the faulting virtual address written to `mtval`/`stval` is the same as would be required for a regular page fault.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: Thus, the faulting virtual address may be a page-boundary address that is higher than the instruction's original virtual address, if the byte at that page boundary is among the accessed bytes.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: When a guest-page fault is not due to an implicit memory access for VS-stage address translation, a nonzero guest physical address written to `mtval2`/`htval` shall correspond to the exact virtual address written to `mtval`/`stval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Guest-Page Faults

chunks.text: When V=0, the virtual-address argument is an HS-level virtual address, and the ASID argument is an HS-level ASID.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: When V=1, the virtual-address argument to SFENCE.VMA is a guest virtual address within the current virtual machine, and the ASID argument is a VS-level ASID within the current virtual machine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: The current virtual machine is identified by the VMID field of CSR `hgatp`, and the effective ASID can be considered to be the combination of this VMID with the VS-level ASID.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: It is noted there that, when PMP settings are modified in a manner that affects either the physical memory that holds page tables or the physical memory to which page tables point, M-mode software must synchronize the PMP settings with the virtual memory system.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: For HS-level address translation, this is accomplished by executing in M-mode an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0`, after the PMP CSRs are written.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: Similarly, if the setting of the PBMTE or ADUE bits in `menvcfg` are changed, an HFENCE.GVMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize with respect to the altered interpretation of G-stage and VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D bit fields, respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: By contrast, if the PBMTE or ADUE bits in `henvcfg` are changed, executing an HFENCE.VVMA with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize with respect to the altered interpretation of VS-stage PTEs' PBMT and A/D bit fields for the currently active VMID.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: Hence, to prevent speculative execution causing one guest's VS-stage translations to be cached under another guest's VMID, world-switch code should zero `vsatp`, then swap `hgatp`, then finally write the new `vsatp` value.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: Similarly, if `henvcfg`.PBMTE/ADUE need be world-switched, they should be switched after zeroing `vsatp` but before writing the new `vsatp` value, obviating the need to execute an HFENCE.VVMA instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Memory-Management Fences

chunks.text: Guest physical addresses (GPAs) are 2 bits wider than the corresponding virtual address translation modes, resulting in additional address translation schemes Sv32x4, Sv39x4, Sv48x4, and Sv57x4 for translating guest physical addresses to supervisor physical addresses.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: When running with virtualization in VS/VU mode with `vsatp.MODE` = Bare, this means that those two bits may be subject to pointer masking, depending on `hgatp.MODE` and `senvcfg.PMM`/`henvcfg.PMM` (for VU/VS mode).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: If `vsatp.MODE` != BARE, this issue does *not* apply.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: To support implementations where (XLEN-PMLEN) can be less than the GPA width supported by `hgatp.MODE`, hypervisors should execute an `HFENCE.GVMA` with rs1=`x0` if the `henvcfg.PMM` is changed from or to a value where (XLEN-PMLEN) is less than GPA width supported by the `hgatp` translation mode of that guest.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: `PMLEN=7` and `hgatp.MODE=sv57x4` * `PMLEN=16` and `hgatp.MODE=sv57x4` * `PMLEN=16` and `hgatp.MODE=sv48x4`
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: Implementation of an address-specific `HFENCE.GVMA` should either ignore the address argument, or should ignore the top masked GPA bits of entries when comparing for an address match.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Two-Stage Address Translation > Interaction with Pointer Masking

chunks.text: The hypervisor extension augments the trap cause encoding. lists the possible M-mode and HS-mode trap cause codes when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: Codes are added for VS-level interrupts (interrupts 2, 6, 10), for supervisor-level guest external interrupts (interrupt 12), for virtual-instruction exceptions (exception 22), and for guest-page faults (exceptions 20, 21, 23).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: An instruction is HS-qualified if it would be valid to execute in HS-mode (for some values of the instruction's register operands), assuming fields TSR and TVM of CSR `mstatus` are both zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: A special rule applies for CSR instructions that access 32-bit high-half CSRs such as `cycleh` and `htimedeltah`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: Specifically, a virtual-instruction exception is raised for the following cases:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode, attempts to access a non-high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in `hcounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access a high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in `hcounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VU-mode, attempts to access a non-high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in either `hcounteren` or `scounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access a high-half counter CSR when the corresponding bit in either `hcounteren` or `scounteren` is 0 and the same bit in `mcounteren` is 1;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode or VU-mode, attempts to access an implemented non-high-half hypervisor CSR or VS CSR when the same access (read/write) would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode or VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access an implemented high-half hypervisor CSR or high-half VS CSR when the same access (read/write) to the CSR"s low-half partner would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VU-mode, attempts to execute WFI when `mstatus`.TW=0, or to execute a supervisor instruction (SRET or SFENCE);
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VU-mode, attempts to access an implemented non-high-half supervisor CSR when the same access (read/write) would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VU-mode, if XLEN=32, attempts to access an implemented high-half supervisor CSR when the same access to the CSR's low-half partner would be allowed in HS-mode, assuming `mstatus`.TVM=0;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute WFI when `hstatus`.VTW=1 and `mstatus`.TW=0, unless the instruction completes within an implementation-specific, bounded time;
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute SRET when `hstatus`.VTSR=1; and
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: in VS-mode, attempts to execute an SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA instruction or to access `satp`, when `hstatus`.VTVM=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: Other extensions to the RISC-V Privileged Architecture may add to the set of circumstances that cause a virtual-instruction exception when V=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: On a virtual-instruction trap, `mtval` or `stval` is written the same as for an illegal-instruction trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: Fields FS and VS in registers `sstatus` and `vsstatus` deviate from the usual HS-qualified rule.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: | .>|12, 20, 1 |During instruction address translation: + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}First encountered page fault, guest-page fault, or access fault
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: | .>|1 |With physical address for instruction: + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}Instruction access fault
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: | |2 + 22 + 0 + 8, 9, 10, 11 + 3 + 3|Illegal instruction + Virtual instruction + Instruction address misaligned + Environment call + Environment break + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}Load/store/AMO address breakpoint
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: | .>|13, 15, 21, 23, 5, 7 |During address translation for an explicit memory access: + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}First encountered page fault, guest-page fault, or access fault
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: | .>|5, 7 |With physical address for an explicit memory access: + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}Load/store/AMO access fault
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: If not higher priority: + {nbsp}{nbsp}{nbsp}Load/store/AMO address misaligned |
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: If an instruction may raise multiple synchronous exceptions, the decreasing priority order of indicates which exception is taken and reported in `mcause` or `scause`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Cause Codes

chunks.text: When a trap occurs in HS-mode or U-mode, it goes to M-mode, unless delegated by `medeleg` or `mideleg`, in which case it goes to HS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: When a trap occurs in VS-mode or VU-mode, it goes to M-mode, unless delegated by `medeleg` or `mideleg`, in which case it goes to HS-mode, unless further delegated by `hedeleg` or `hideleg`, in which case it goes to VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, virtualization mode V gets set to 0, and fields MPV and MPP in `mstatus` (or `mstatush`) are set according to . A trap into M-mode also writes fields GVA, MPIE, and MIE in `mstatus`/`mstatush` and writes CSRs `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mtval2`, and `mtinst`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry
