chunks.text: Upon trap return, MPV is ignored when MPP=3. | |Previous Mode |MPV |MPP |U-mode + HS-mode + M-mode|0 + 0 + 0|0 + 1 + 3 |VU-mode + VS-mode|1 + 1|0 + 1 |
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into HS-mode, virtualization mode V is set to 0, and `hstatus`.SPV and `sstatus`.SPP are set according to .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: If V was 1 before the trap, field SPVP in `hstatus` is set the same as `sstatus`.SPP; otherwise, SPVP is left unchanged. A trap into HS-mode also writes field GVA in `hstatus`, fields SPIE and SIE in `sstatus`, and CSRs `sepc`, `scause`, `stval`, `htval`, and `htinst`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: When a trap is taken into VS-mode, `vsstatus`.SPP is set according to .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: Register `hstatus` and the HS-level `sstatus` are not modified, and the virtualization mode V remains 1. A trap into VS-mode also writes fields SPIE and SIE in `vsstatus` and writes CSRs `vsepc`, `vscause`, and `vstval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Entry

chunks.text: On any trap into M-mode or HS-mode, one of these values is written automatically into the appropriate trap instruction CSR, `mtinst` or `htinst`:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: zero; * a transformation of the trapping instruction; * a custom value (allowed only if the trapping instruction is non-standard); or * a special pseudoinstruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Except when a pseudoinstruction value is required (described later), the value written to `mtinst` or `htinst` may always be zero, indicating that the hardware is providing no information in the register for this particular trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: On an interrupt, the value written to the trap instruction register is always zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: On a synchronous exception, if a nonzero value is written, one of the following shall be true about the value:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: The instruction that trapped is a non-standard instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: These three cases exclude a large number of other possible values, such as all those having bits 1:0 equal to binary `10`. A future standard or extension may define additional cases, thus allowing values that are currently excluded.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Software may safely treat an unrecognized value in a trap instruction register the same as zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: shows the values that may be automatically written to the trap instruction register for each standard exception cause.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: For exceptions that prevent the fetching of an instruction, only zero or a pseudoinstruction value may be written. A custom value may be automatically written only if the instruction that traps is non-standard. A future standard or extension may permit other values to be written, chosen from the set of allowed values established earlier.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: As enumerated in the table, a synchronous exception may write to the trap instruction register a standard transformation of the trapping instruction only for exceptions that arise from explicit memory accesses (from loads, stores, and AMO instructions).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: If a synchronous trap occurs for a standard instruction for which no transformation has been defined, the trap instruction register shall be written with zero (or, under certain circumstances, with a special pseudoinstruction value).
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: All fields are the same as the trapping instruction except bits 19:15, Addr.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: All fields are the same as the trapping instruction except bits 19:15, Addr.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Offset field that replaces the instruction’s rs1 field in bits 19:15 is the positive difference between the faulting virtual address (written to `mtval` or `stval`) and the original virtual address.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: For a standard compressed instruction (16-bit size), the transformed instruction is found as follows:
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Expand the compressed instruction to its 32-bit equivalent. .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Transform the 32-bit equivalent instruction. .
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: Bits 1:0 of a transformed standard instruction will be binary `01` if the trapping instruction is compressed and `11` if not.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: For guest-page faults, the trap instruction register is written with a special pseudoinstruction value if: (a) the fault is caused by an implicit memory access for VS-stage address translation, and (b) a nonzero value (the faulting guest physical address) is written to `mtval2` or `htval`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: If both conditions are met, the value written to `mtinst` or `htinst` must be taken from ; zero is not allowed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: The RV32 values are used when VSXLEN=32, and the RV64 values when VSXLEN=64. | |Value |Meaning |`0x00002000` + `0x00002020` |32-bit read for VS-stage address translation (RV32) + 32-bit write for VS-stage address translation (RV32)
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: |`0x00003000` + `0x00003020` |64-bit read for VS-stage address translation (RV64) + 64-bit write for VS-stage address translation (RV64) |
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: If a machine never automatically updates bits A or D in VS-level page tables (leaving this to software), the write case will never arise.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: The fact that such a page table update must actually be atomic, not just a simple write, is ignored for the pseudoinstruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Transformed Instruction or Pseudoinstruction for `mtinst` or `htinst`

chunks.text: The MRET instruction is used to return from a trap taken into M-mode. MRET first determines what the new privilege mode will be according to the values of MPP and MPV in `mstatus` or `mstatush`, as encoded in . MRET then in `mstatus`/`mstatush` sets MPV=0, MPP=0, MIE=MPIE, and MPIE=1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: Lastly, MRET sets the privilege mode as previously determined, and sets `pc`=`mepc`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: The SRET instruction is used to return from a trap taken into HS-mode or VS-mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: Lastly, SRET sets the privilege mode as previously determined, and sets `pc`=`sepc`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: If the Ssdbltrp extension is implemented, when `SRET` is executed in HS-mode, if the new privilege mode is VU, the `SRET` instruction sets `vsstatus.SDT` to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: When executed in VS-mode, `vsstatus.SDT` is set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "H" Extension for Hypervisor Support, Version 1.0 > Traps > Trap Return

chunks.text: In addition to the machine-level CSRs described in this section, M-mode code can access all CSRs at lower privilege levels.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs

chunks.text: The `misa` CSR is a *WARL* read-write register reporting the ISA supported by the hart.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: This register must be readable in any implementation, but a value of zero can be returned to indicate the `misa` register has not been implemented, requiring that CPU capabilities be determined through a separate non-standard mechanism.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The MXL (Machine XLEN) field encodes the native base integer ISA width as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The MXL field is read-only.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: If `misa` is nonzero, the MXL field indicates the effective XLEN in M-mode, a constant termed MXLEN. XLEN is never greater than MXLEN, but XLEN might be smaller than MXLEN in less-privileged modes.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The `misa` CSR is MXLEN bits wide.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The Extensions field encodes the presence of the standard extensions, with a single bit per letter of the alphabet (bit 0 encodes presence of extension "A" , bit 1 encodes presence of extension "B", through to bit 25 which encodes "Z").
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The "I" bit will be set for the RV32I and RV64I base ISAs, and the "E" bit will be set for RV32E and RV64E.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The Extensions field is a *WARL* field that can contain writable bits where the implementation allows the supported ISA to be modified.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: At reset, the Extensions field shall contain the maximal set of supported extensions, and "I" shall be selected over "E" if both are available.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When a standard extension is disabled by clearing its bit in `misa`, the instructions and CSRs defined or modified by the extension revert to their defined or reserved behaviors as if the extension is not implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: All bits that are reserved for future use must return zero when read.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The "X" bit will be set if there are any non-standard extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "B" bit is 1, the implementation supports the instructions provided by the Zba, Zbb, and Zbs extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "B" bit is 0, it indicates that the implementation might not support one or more of the Zba, Zbb, or Zbs extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "M" bit is 1, the implementation supports all multiply and division instructions defined by the M extension.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "M" bit is 0, it indicates that the implementation might not support those instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: However if the Zmmul extension is supported then the multiply instructions it specifies are supported irrespective of the value of the "M" bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "S" bit is 1, the implementation supports supervisor mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "S" bit is 0, the implementation might not support supervisor mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "U" bit is 1, the implementation supports user mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When the "U" bit is 0, the implementation might not support user mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: Unless `misa` is all read-only zero, the "E" bit always reads as the complement of the "I" bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: If an execution environment supports both RV32E and RV32I, software can select RV32E by clearing the "I" bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: If an ISA feature x depends on an ISA feature y, then attempting to enable feature x but disable feature y results in both features being disabled.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: Similarly, setting "U"=0 and "S"=1" results in both "U" and "S" being cleared.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: An implementation may impose additional constraints on the collective setting of two or more `misa` fields, in which case they function collectively as a single *WARL* field.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: An attempt to write an unsupported combination causes those bits to be set to some supported combination.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: If an instruction that would write `misa` increases IALIGN, and the subsequent instruction's address is not IALIGN-bit aligned, the write to `misa` is suppressed, leaving `misa` unchanged.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: When software enables an extension that was previously disabled, then all state uniquely associated with that extension is UNSPECIFIED, unless otherwise specified by that extension.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: This follows from the fact that, when a feature is not implemented, the corresponding opcodes and CSRs become reserved, not necessarily illegal.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine ISA (`misa`) Register

chunks.text: The `mvendorid` CSR is a 32-bit read-only register providing the JEDEC manufacturer ID of the provider of the core.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Vendor ID (`mvendorid`) Register

chunks.text: This register must be readable in any implementation, but a value of 0 can be returned to indicate the field is not implemented or that this is a non-commercial implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Vendor ID (`mvendorid`) Register

chunks.text: JEDEC manufacturer IDs are ordinarily encoded as a sequence of one-byte continuation codes `0x7f`, terminated by a one-byte ID not equal to `0x7f`, with an odd parity bit in the most-significant bit of each byte. `mvendorid` encodes the number of one-byte continuation codes in the Bank field, and encodes the final byte in the Offset field, discarding the parity bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Vendor ID (`mvendorid`) Register

chunks.text: The `marchid` CSR is an MXLEN-bit read-only register encoding the base microarchitecture of the hart.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Architecture ID (`marchid`) Register

chunks.text: This register must be readable in any implementation, but a value of 0 can be returned to indicate the field is not implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Architecture ID (`marchid`) Register

chunks.text: The combination of `mvendorid` and `marchid` should uniquely identify the type of hart microarchitecture that is implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Architecture ID (`marchid`) Register

chunks.text: Open-source project architecture IDs are allocated globally by RISC-V International, and have non-zero architecture IDs with a zero most-significant-bit (MSB).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Architecture ID (`marchid`) Register

chunks.text: Commercial architecture IDs are allocated by each commercial vendor independently, but must have the MSB set and cannot contain zero in the remaining MXLEN-1 bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Architecture ID (`marchid`) Register

chunks.text: The `mimpid` CSR provides a unique encoding of the version of the processor implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Implementation ID (`mimpid`) Register

chunks.text: This register must be readable in any implementation, but a value of 0 can be returned to indicate that the field is not implemented.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Implementation ID (`mimpid`) Register

chunks.text: The Implementation value should reflect the design of the RISC-V processor itself and not any surrounding system.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Implementation ID (`mimpid`) Register

chunks.text: The `mhartid` CSR is an MXLEN-bit read-only register containing the integer ID of the hardware thread running the code.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hart ID (`mhartid`) Register

chunks.text: This register must be readable in any implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hart ID (`mhartid`) Register

chunks.text: Hart IDs might not necessarily be numbered contiguously in a multiprocessor system, but one hart must have a hart ID of zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hart ID (`mhartid`) Register

chunks.text: Hart IDs must be unique within the execution environment.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hart ID (`mhartid`) Register

chunks.text: The `mstatus` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in for RV32 and for RV64.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: The `mstatus` register keeps track of and controls the hart’s current operating state. A restricted view of `mstatus` appears as the `sstatus` register in the S-level ISA.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: For RV32 only, `mstatush` is a 32-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Bits 30:4 of `mstatush` generally contain the same fields found in bits 62:36 of `mstatus` for RV64.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Fields SD, SXL, and UXL do not exist in `mstatush`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers

chunks.text: Global interrupt-enable bits, MIE and SIE, are provided for M-mode and S-mode respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: These bits are primarily used to guarantee atomicity with respect to interrupt handlers in the current privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: When a hart is executing in privilege mode x, interrupts are globally enabled when xIE=1 and globally disabled when xIE=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: Interrupts for lower-privilege modes, w<x, are always globally disabled regardless of the setting of any global wIE bit for the lower-privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: Interrupts for higher-privilege modes, y>x, are always globally enabled regardless of the setting of the global yIE bit for the higher-privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: Higher-privilege-level code can use separate per-interrupt enable bits to disable selected higher-privilege-mode interrupts before ceding control to a lower-privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: If supervisor mode is not implemented, then SIE and SPIE are read-only 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: To support nested traps, each privilege mode x that can respond to interrupts has a two-level stack of interrupt-enable bits and privilege modes. xPIE holds the value of the interrupt-enable bit active prior to the trap, and xPP holds the previous privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: The xPP fields can only hold privilege modes up to x, so MPP is two bits wide and SPP is one bit wide.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: When a trap is taken from privilege mode y into privilege mode x, xPIE is set to the value of xIE; xIE is set to 0; and xPP is set to y.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: An MRET or SRET instruction is used to return from a trap in M-mode or S-mode respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: When executing an xRET instruction, supposing xPP holds the value y, xIE is set to xPIE; the privilege mode is changed to y; xPIE is set to 1; and xPP is set to the least-privileged supported mode (U if U-mode is implemented, else M).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: If y{ne}M, xRET also sets MPRV=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: xPP fields are *WARL* fields that can hold only privilege mode x and any implemented privilege mode lower than x.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: If privilege mode x is not implemented, then xPP must be read-only 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Privilege and Global Interrupt-Enable Stack in `mstatus` register

chunks.text: This non-reentrancy usually occurs in the early phase of trap handling, wherein the trap handler has not yet preserved the necessary state to handle and resume from the trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The occurrence of a trap during this phase can lead to an overwrite of critical state information, resulting in the loss of data needed to recover from the initial trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The trap that caused this critical error condition is henceforth called the unexpected trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: However, managing Hardware-Error exceptions, which may occur unpredictably, presents significant challenges in trap handler implementation due to the potential risk of a double trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The M-mode-disable-trap (`MDT`) bit is a WARL field introduced by the Smdbltrp extension.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Upon reset, the `MDT` field is set to 1.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the `MDT` bit is set to 1 by an explicit CSR write, the `MIE` (Machine Interrupt Enable) bit is cleared to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: For RV64, this clearing occurs regardless of the value written, if any, to the `MIE` bit by the same write.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The `MIE` bit can only be set to 1 by an explicit CSR write if the `MDT` bit is already 0 or, for RV64, is being set to 0 by the same write (For RV32, the `MDT` bit is in `mstatush` and the `MIE` bit in `mstatus` register).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When a trap is to be taken into M-mode, if the `MDT` bit is currently 0, it is then set to 1, and the trap is delivered as expected.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: However, if `MDT` is already set to 1, then this is an unexpected trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the Smrnmi extension is implemented, a trap caused by an RNMI is not considered an unexpected trap irrespective of the state of the `MDT` bit. A trap caused by an RNMI does not set the `MDT` bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: However, a trap that occurs when executing in M-mode with `mnstatus.NMIE` set to 0 is an unexpected trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In the event of a unexpected trap, the handling is as follows:
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the Smrnmi extension is implemented and `mnstatus.NMIE` is 1, the hart traps to the RNMI handler.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: To deliver this trap, the `mnepc` and `mncause` registers are written with the values that the unexpected trap would have written to the `mepc` and `mcause` registers respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The privilege mode information fields in the `mnstatus` register are written to indicate M-mode and its `NMIE` field is set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the Smrnmi extension is not implemented, or if the Smrnmi extension is implemented and `mnstatus.NMIE` is 0, the hart enters a critical-error state without updating any architectural state, including the `pc`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: This state involves ceasing execution, disabling all interrupts (including NMIs), and asserting a `critical-error` signal to the platform.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Whether performance counters and timers are updated in the critical-error state is UNSPECIFIED.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the Smdbltrp extension is implemented, executing an `MRET` instruction, or executing an `SRET` instruction while the current privilege mode is M, has the following additional effects.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The `MDT` bit is set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If the Ssdbltrp extension is also implemented, and the new privilege mode is U, VS, or VU, then `sstatus.SDT` is also set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Additionally, if it is VU, then `vsstatus.SDT` is also set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the Smdbltrp extension is implemented, the `MNRET` instruction, provided by the Smrnmi extension, sets the `MDT` bit to 0 if the new privilege mode is not M.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If the Ssdbltrp extension is also implemented, and the new privilege mode is U, VS, or VU, then `sstatus.SDT` is also set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Additionally, if it is VU, then `vsstatus.SDT` is also set to 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Double Trap Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: For RV64 harts, the SXL and UXL fields are *WARL* fields that control the value of XLEN for S-mode and U-mode, respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The encoding of these fields is the same as the MXL field of `misa`, shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The effective XLEN in S-mode and U-mode are termed SXLEN and UXLEN, respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MXLEN=32, the SXL and UXL fields do not exist, and SXLEN=32 and UXLEN=32.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MXLEN=64, if S-mode is not supported, then SXL is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Otherwise, it is a *WARL* field that encodes the current value of SXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In particular, an implementation may make SXL be a read-only field whose value always ensures that SXLEN=MXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MXLEN=64, if U-mode is not supported, then UXL is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Otherwise, it is a *WARL* field that encodes the current value of UXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In particular, an implementation may make UXL be a read-only field whose value always ensures that UXLEN=MXLEN or UXLEN=SXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If S-mode is implemented, the set of legal values that the UXL field may assume excludes those that would cause UXLEN to be greater than SXLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Whenever XLEN in any mode is set to a value less than the widest supported XLEN, all operations must ignore source operand register bits above the configured XLEN, and must sign-extend results to fill the entire widest supported XLEN in the destination register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Similarly, `pc` bits above XLEN are ignored, and when the `pc` is written, it is sign-extended to fill the widest supported XLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When such a HINT is executed with XLEN < MXLEN and bits MXLEN..XLEN of the destination register not all equal to bit XLEN-1, it is implementation-defined whether bits MXLEN..XLEN of the destination register are unchanged or are overwritten with copies of bit XLEN-1.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The implementation choice is observable only by privilege modes with an XLEN setting greater than the current XLEN; it is invisible to the current privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Base ISA Control in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MPRV=0, explicit memory accesses behave as normal, using the translation and protection mechanisms of the current privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MPRV=1, load and store memory addresses are translated and protected, and endianness is applied, as though the current privilege mode were set to MPP.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Instruction address-translation and protection are unaffected by the setting of MPRV. MPRV is read-only 0 if U-mode is not supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: An MRET or SRET instruction that changes the privilege mode to a mode less privileged than M also sets MPRV=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The MXR (Make eXecutable Readable) bit modifies the privilege with which loads access virtual memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MXR=0, only loads from pages marked readable (R=1 in ) will succeed.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When MXR=1, loads from pages marked either readable or executable (R=1 or X=1) will succeed. MXR has no effect when page-based virtual memory is not in effect. MXR is read-only 0 if S-mode is not supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The SUM (permit Supervisor User Memory access) bit modifies the privilege with which S-mode loads and stores access virtual memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When SUM=0, S-mode memory accesses to pages that are accessible by U-mode (U=1 in ) will fault.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When SUM=1, these accesses are permitted. SUM has no effect when page-based virtual memory is not in effect.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The MXR and SUM mechanisms only affect the interpretation of permissions encoded in page-table entries.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In particular, they have no impact on whether access-fault exceptions are raised due to PMAs or PMP.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Memory Privilege in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The MBE, SBE, and UBE bits in `mstatus` and `mstatush` are *WARL* fields that control the endianness of memory accesses other than instruction fetches.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: Instruction fetches are always little-endian.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: MBE controls whether non-instruction-fetch memory accesses made from M-mode (assuming `mstatus`.MPRV=0) are little-endian (MBE=0) or big-endian (MBE=1).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: If S-mode is not supported, SBE is read-only 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: Otherwise, SBE controls whether explicit load and store memory accesses made from S-mode are little-endian (SBE=0) or big-endian (SBE=1).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: If U-mode is not supported, UBE is read-only 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: Otherwise, UBE controls whether explicit load and store memory accesses made from U-mode are little-endian (UBE=0) or big-endian (UBE=1).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: For implicit accesses to supervisor-level memory management data structures, such as page tables, endianness is always controlled by SBE.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: Since changing SBE alters the implementation’s interpretation of these data structures, if any such data structures remain in use across a change to SBE, M-mode software must follow such a change to SBE by executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: If S-mode is supported, an implementation may make SBE be a read-only copy of MBE.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: If U-mode is supported, an implementation may make UBE be a read-only copy of either MBE or SBE.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Endianness Control in `mstatus` and `mstatush` Registers

chunks.text: The TVM (Trap Virtual Memory) bit is a *WARL* field that supports intercepting supervisor virtual-memory management operations.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TVM=1, attempts to read or write the `satp` CSR or execute an SFENCE.VMA or SINVAL.VMA instruction while executing in S-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TVM=0, these operations are permitted in S-mode. TVM is read-only 0 when S-mode is not supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TW=0, the WFI instruction may execute in modes less privileged than M when not prevented for some other reason.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TW=1, then if WFI is executed in any less-privileged mode, and it does not complete within an implementation-specific, bounded time limit, the WFI instruction causes an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: An implementation may have WFI always raise an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M when TW=1, even if there are pending globally-disabled interrupts when the instruction is executed. TW is read-only 0 when there are no modes less privileged than M.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When S-mode is implemented, then executing WFI in U-mode causes an illegal-instruction exception, regardless of the value of the TW bit, unless the instruction completes within an implementation-specific, bounded time limit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The TSR (Trap SRET) bit is a *WARL* field that supports intercepting the supervisor exception return instruction, SRET.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TSR=1, attempts to execute SRET while executing in S-mode will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When TSR=0, this operation is permitted in S-mode. TSR is read-only 0 when S-mode is not supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Virtualization Support in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Supporting substantial extensions is one of the primary goals of RISC-V, and hence we define a standard interface to allow unchanged privileged-mode code, particularly a supervisor-level OS, to support arbitrary user-mode state extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The FS[1:0] and VS[1:0] *WARL* fields and the XS[1:0] read-only field are used to reduce the cost of context save and restore by setting and tracking the current state of the floating-point unit and any other user-mode extensions respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The FS field encodes the status of the floating-point unit state, including the floating-point registers `f0`–`f31` and the CSRs `fcsr`, `frm`, and `fflags`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The VS field encodes the status of the vector extension state, including the vector registers `v0`–`v31` and the CSRs `vcsr`, `vxrm`, `vxsat`, `vstart`, `vl`, `vtype`, and `vlenb`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The XS field encodes the status of additional user-mode extensions and associated state.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: These fields can be checked by a context switch routine to quickly determine whether a state save or restore is required.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The FS, VS, and XS fields use the same status encoding as shown in , with the four possible status values being Off, Initial, Clean, and Dirty.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If the F extension is implemented, the FS field shall not be read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If neither the F extension nor S-mode is implemented, then FS is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If S-mode is implemented but the F extension is not, FS may optionally be read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If the `v` registers are implemented, the VS field shall not be read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If neither the `v` registers nor S-mode is implemented, then VS is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If S-mode is implemented but the `v` registers are not, VS may optionally be read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In harts without additional user extensions requiring new state, the XS field is read-only zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Every additional extension with state provides a CSR field that encodes the equivalent of the XS states.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The XS field represents a summary of all extensions' status as shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The SD bit is a read-only bit that summarizes whether either the FS, VS, or XS fields signal the presence of some dirty state that will require saving extended user context to memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If FS, XS, and VS are all read-only zero, then SD is also always zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When an extension's status is set to Off, any instruction that attempts to read or write the corresponding state will cause an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the status is Initial, the corresponding state should have an initial constant value.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the status is Clean, the corresponding state is potentially different from the initial value, but matches the last value stored on a context swap.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When the status is Dirty, the corresponding state has potentially been modified since the last context save.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: During a context save, the responsible privileged code need only write out the corresponding state if its status is Dirty, and can then reset the extension's status to Clean.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: During a context restore, the context need only be loaded from memory if the status is Clean (it should never be Dirty at restore).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If the status is Initial, the context must be set to an initial constant value on context restore to avoid a security hole, but this can be done without accessing memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The FS and XS fields are read by the privileged code before saving the context.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The FS field is set directly by privileged code when resuming a user context, while the XS field is set indirectly by writing to the status register of the individual extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The status fields will also be updated during execution of instructions, regardless of privilege mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Extensions to the user-mode ISA often include additional user-mode state, and this state can be considerably larger than the base integer registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The extensions might only be used for some applications, or might only be needed for short phases within a single application.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: To improve performance, the user-mode extension can define additional instructions to allow user-mode software to return the unit to an initial state or even to turn off the unit.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The unconfigured state would be represented as the Initial state for context save.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Executing a user-mode instruction to disable a unit and place it into the Off state will cause an illegal-instruction exception to be raised if any subsequent instruction tries to use the unit before it is turned back on. A user-mode instruction to turn a unit on must also ensure the unit's state is properly initialized, as the unit might have been used by another context meantime.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Changing the setting of FS has no effect on the contents of the floating-point register state.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: In particular, setting FS=Off does not destroy the state, nor does setting FS=Initial clear the contents.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Similarly, the setting of VS has no effect on the contents of the vector register state.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Other extensions, however, might not preserve state when set to Off.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Implementations may choose to track the dirtiness of the floating-point register state imprecisely by reporting the state to be dirty even when it has not been modified.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: On some implementations, some instructions that do not mutate the floating-point state may cause the state to transition from Initial or Clean to Dirty.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: On other implementations, dirtiness might not be tracked at all, in which case the valid FS states are Off and Dirty, and an attempt to set FS to Initial or Clean causes it to be set to Dirty.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: If an instruction explicitly or implicitly writes a floating-point register or the `fcsr` but does not alter its contents, and FS=Initial or FS=Clean, it is implementation-defined whether FS transitions to Dirty.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Implementations may choose to track the dirtiness of the vector register state in an analogous imprecise fashion, including possibly setting VS to Dirty when software attempts to set VS=Initial or VS=Clean.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: When VS=Initial or VS=Clean, it is implementation-defined whether an instruction that writes a vector register or vector CSR but does not alter its contents causes VS to transition to Dirty.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: shows all the possible state transitions for the FS, VS, or XS status bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|At context save in privileged code
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Save state? + Next state |No + Off |No + Initial |No + Clean |Yes + Clean |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|At context restore in privileged code
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Restore state? + Next state |No + Off |Yes, to initial + Initial |Yes, from memory + Clean |N/A + N/A |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|Execute instruction to read state
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Action? + Next state |Exception + Off |Execute + Initial |Execute + Clean |Execute + Dirty |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|Execute instruction that possibly modifies state, including configuration
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Action? + Next state |Exception + Off |Execute + Dirty |Execute + Dirty |Execute + Dirty |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|Execute instruction to unconfigure unit
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Action? + Next state |Exception + Off |Execute + Initial |Execute + Initial |Execute + Initial |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|Execute instruction to disable unit
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Action? + Next state |Execute + Off |Execute + Off |Execute + Off |Execute + Off |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: [width=75,align=center,float=center,cols="<,<,<,<,<"] | 5+^|Execute instruction to enable unit
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: |Action? + Next state |Execute + Initial |Execute + Initial |Execute + Initial |Execute + Initial |
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Standard privileged instructions to initialize, save, and restore extension state are provided to insulate privileged code from details of the added extension state by treating the state as an opaque object.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The XS field provides a summary of all added extension state, but additional microarchitectural bits might be maintained in the extension to further reduce context save and restore overhead.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The floating-point unit state is always initialized, saved, and restored using standard instructions (F, D, and/or Q), and privileged code must be aware of FLEN to determine the appropriate space to reserve for each `f` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Machine and Supervisor modes share a single copy of the FS, VS, and XS bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Supervisor-level software normally uses the FS, VS, and XS bits directly to record the status with respect to the supervisor-level saved context.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: Machine-level software must be more conservative in saving and restoring the extension state in their corresponding version of the context.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `SPELP` and `MPELP` fields that hold the previous `ELP`, and are updated as specified in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The *x*`PELP` fields are encoded as follows:
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `mstatus` Register

chunks.text: The `mtvec` register is an MXLEN-bit *WARL* read/write register that holds trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE) and a vector mode (MODE).
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: The `mtvec` register must always be implemented, but can contain a read-only value.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: If `mtvec` is writable, the set of values the register may hold can vary by implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: The value in the BASE field must always be aligned on a 4-byte boundary, and the MODE setting may impose additional alignment constraints on the value in the BASE field.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: When used as an address, the lower two bits are filled with zeroes to obtain an XLEN-bit address that is always aligned on a 4-byte boundary.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: The encoding of the MODE field is shown in .
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: When MODE=Direct, all traps into machine mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: When MODE=Vectored, all synchronous exceptions into machine mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field, whereas interrupts cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field plus four times the interrupt cause number.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: An implementation may have different alignment constraints for different modes.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: In particular, MODE=Vectored may have stricter alignment constraints than MODE=Direct.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

chunks.text: By default, all traps at any privilege level are handled in machine mode, though a machine-mode handler can redirect traps back to the appropriate level with the MRET instruction ().
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

chunks.text: To increase performance, implementations can provide individual read/write bits within `medeleg` and `mideleg` to indicate that certain exceptions and interrupts should be processed directly by a lower privilege level.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

chunks.text: The machine exception delegation register (`medeleg`) is a 64-bit read/write register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

chunks.text: The machine interrupt delegation (`mideleg`) register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

chunks.text: In harts with S-mode, the `medeleg` and `mideleg` registers must exist, and setting a bit in `medeleg` or `mideleg` will delegate the corresponding trap, when occurring in S-mode or U-mode, to the S-mode trap handler.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

chunks.text: In harts without S-mode, the `medeleg` and `mideleg` registers should not exist.
chunks.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers
