chunks.text: Implementations may also change `mstatus.VS` or `vsstatus.VS` from Initial or Clean to Dirty at any time, even when there is no change in vector state.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Context Status in `vsstatus`

chunks.text: If `vsstatus.VS` is Dirty, `vsstatus.SD` is 1; otherwise, `vsstatus.SD` is set in accordance with existing specifications.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Context Status in `vsstatus`

chunks.text: If `mstatus.VS` is Dirty, `mstatus.SD` is 1; otherwise, `mstatus.SD` is set in accordance with existing specifications.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Context Status in `vsstatus`

chunks.text: For implementations with a writable `misa.V` field, the `vsstatus.VS` field may exist even if `misa.V` is clear.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Context Status in `vsstatus`

chunks.text: The read-only XLEN-wide vector type CSR, `vtype` provides the default type used to interpret the contents of the vector register file, and can only be updated by `vset{i}vl{i}` instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: The vector type determines the organization of elements in each vector register, and how multiple vector registers are grouped.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: The `vtype` register also indicates how masked-off elements and elements past the current vector length in a vector result are handled.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: The `vtype` register has five fields, `vill`, `vma`, `vta`, `vsew[2:0]`, and `vlmul[2:0]`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: Bits `vtype[XLEN-2:8]` should be written with zero, and non-zero values in this field are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: The illegal value represented by `vill` can be internally encoded using the illegal 64-bit combination in `vsew[1:0]` without requiring an additional storage bit to hold `vill`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: In many cases, the `vl` and `vtype` values can be reused across multiple instructions, reducing the static and dynamic instruction overhead from the `vset{i}vl{i}` instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: It is anticipated that a future extended 64-bit instruction encoding would allow these fields to be specified statically in the instruction encoding.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register

chunks.text: The value in `vsew` sets the dynamic selected element width (SEW).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: By default, a vector register is viewed as being divided into VLEN/SEW elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The supported element width may vary with LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Some future extensions may support larger SEWs only when bits from multiple vector registers are combined using LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: In this case, software that relies on large SEW should attempt to use the largest LMUL, and hence the fewest vector register groups, to increase the number of implementations on which the code will run.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The `vill` bit in `vtype` should be checked after setting `vtype` to see if the configuration is supported, and an alternate code path should be provided if it is not.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Alternatively, a profile can mandate the minimum SEW at each LMUL setting.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Selected Element Width (`vsew[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Multiple vector registers can be grouped together, so that a single vector instruction can operate on multiple vector registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The term vector register group is used herein to refer to one or more vector registers used as a single operand to a vector instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The vector length multiplier, LMUL, when greater than 1, represents the default number of vector registers that are combined to form a vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Implementations must support LMUL integer values of 1, 2, 4, and 8.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The effective LMUL (EMUL) of each vector operand is determined by the number of registers required to hold the elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: LMUL can also be a fractional value, reducing the number of bits used in a single vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Fractional LMUL is used to increase the number of effective usable vector register groups when operating on mixed-width values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: This can limit the number of vector register groups available.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: With fractional LMUL, the widest values need occupy only a single vector register while narrower values can occupy a fraction of a single vector register, allowing all 32 architectural vector register names to be used for different values in a vector loop even when handling mixed-width values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Fractional LMUL implies portions of vector registers are unused, but in some cases, having more shorter register-resident vectors improves efficiency relative to fewer longer register-resident vectors.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Implementations must provide fractional LMUL settings that allow the narrowest supported type to occupy a fraction of a vector register corresponding to the ratio of the narrowest supported type's width to that of the largest supported type's width.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: In the standard extensions, SEW~MIN~=8.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: For standard vector extensions with ELEN=32, fractional LMULs of 1/2 and 1/4 must be supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: For standard vector extensions with ELEN=64, fractional LMULs of 1/2, 1/4, and 1/8 must be supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: When LMUL < SEW~MIN~/ELEN, there is no guarantee an implementation would have enough bits in the fractional vector register to store at least one element, as VLEN=ELEN is a valid implementation choice.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: For a given supported fractional LMUL setting, implementations must support SEW settings between SEW~MIN~ and LMUL * ELEN, inclusive.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The use of `vtype` encodings with LMUL < SEW~MIN~/ELEN is reserved, but implementations can set `vill` if they do not support these configurations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: The derived value VLMAX = LMUL*VLEN/SEW represents the maximum number of elements that can be operated on with a single vector instruction given the current SEW and LMUL settings as shown in the table below.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Instructions specifying an LMUL=2 vector register group with an odd-numbered vector register are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: When LMUL=4, the vector register group contains four vector registers, and instructions specifying an LMUL=4 vector register group using vector register numbers that are not multiples of four are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: When LMUL=8, the vector register group contains eight vector registers, and instructions specifying an LMUL=8 vector register group using register numbers that are not multiples of eight are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: Mask registers are always contained in a single vector register, regardless of LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Register Grouping (`vlmul[2:0]`)

chunks.text: These two bits modify the behavior of destination tail elements and destination inactive masked-off elements respectively during the execution of vector instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The tail and inactive sets contain element positions that are not receiving new results during a vector operation,
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: All systems must support all four options:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: [cols="1,1,3,3"] | | `vta` | `vma` | Tail Elements | Inactive Elements
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: | 0 | 0 | undisturbed | undisturbed | 0 | 1 | undisturbed | agnostic | 1 | 0 | agnostic | undisturbed | 1 | 1 | agnostic | agnostic |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: Mask destination tail elements are always treated as tail-agnostic, regardless of the setting of `vta`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: When a set is marked undisturbed, the corresponding set of destination elements in a vector register group retain the value they previously held.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: When a set is marked agnostic, the corresponding set of destination elements in any vector destination operand can either retain the value they previously held, or are overwritten with 1s.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: Within a single vector instruction, each destination element can be either left undisturbed or overwritten with 1s, in any combination, and the pattern of undisturbed or overwritten with 1s is not required to be deterministic when the instruction is executed with the same inputs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: With an undisturbed policy, all elements would have to be read from the old physical destination vector register to be copied into the new physical destination vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: This causes an inefficiency when these inactive or tail values are not required for subsequent calculations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The `vta` and `vma` state bits must still be provided in `vtype` for compatibility and to support thread migration.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: As it might be necessary to restart in the middle, we allow arbitrary mixing of agnostic policies within a single vector instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: In addition, except for mask load instructions, any element in the tail of a mask result can also be written with the value the mask-producing operation would have calculated with `vl`=VLMAX.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: There appears to be little software need to support tail-undisturbed for mask register values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: Allowing mask-generating instructions to write back the result of the instruction avoids the need for logic to mask out the tail, except mask loads cannot write memory values to destination mask tails as this would imply accessing memory past software intent.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The assembly syntax adds two mandatory flags to the `vsetvli` instruction:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: ---- ta Tail agnostic tu Tail undisturbed ma Mask agnostic mu Mask undisturbed
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m4, ta, ma Tail agnostic, mask agnostic vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m4, tu, ma Tail undisturbed, mask agnostic vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m4, ta, mu Tail agnostic, mask undisturbed vsetvli t0, a0, e32, m4, tu, mu Tail undisturbed, mask undisturbed ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The use of `vsetvli` without these flags is deprecated, however, and specifying a flag setting is now mandatory.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The default should perhaps be tail-agnostic/mask-agnostic, so software has to specify when it cares about the non-participating elements, but given the historical meaning of the instruction prior to introduction of these flags, it was decided to always require them in future assembly code.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Tail Agnostic and Vector Mask Agnostic `vta` and `vma`

chunks.text: The `vill` bit is used to encode that a previous `vset{i}vl{i}` instruction attempted to write an unsupported value to `vtype`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Type Illegal (`vill`)

chunks.text: If the `vill` bit is set, then any attempt to execute a vector instruction that depends upon `vtype` will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Type Illegal (`vill`)

chunks.text: When the `vill` bit is set, the other XLEN-1 bits in `vtype` shall be zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Type (`vtype`) Register > Vector Type Illegal (`vill`)

chunks.text: The XLEN-bit-wide read-only `vl` CSR can only be updated by the `vset{i}vl{i}` instructions, and the fault-only-first vector load instruction variants.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Length (`vl`) Register

chunks.text: The `vl` register holds an unsigned integer specifying the number of elements to be updated with results from a vector instruction, as further detailed in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Length (`vl`) Register

chunks.text: The smallest vector implementation with VLEN=32 and supporting SEW=8 would need at least six bits in `vl` to hold the values 0-32 (VLEN=32, with LMUL=8 and SEW=8, yields VLMAX=32).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Length (`vl`) Register

chunks.text: The XLEN-bit-wide read-write `vstart` CSR specifies the index of the first element to be executed by a vector instruction, as described in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: Normally, `vstart` is only written by hardware on a trap on a vector instruction, with the `vstart` value representing the element on which the trap was taken (either a synchronous exception or an asynchronous interrupt), and at which execution should resume after a resumable trap is handled.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: All vector instructions are defined to begin execution with the element number given in the `vstart` CSR, leaving earlier elements in the destination vector undisturbed, and to reset the `vstart` CSR to zero at the end of execution.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: `vstart` is not modified by vector instructions that raise illegal-instruction exceptions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: The `vstart` CSR is defined to have only enough writable bits to hold the largest element index (one less than the maximum VLMAX).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: The use of `vstart` values greater than the largest element index for the current `vtype` setting is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: It is not required to trap, as a possible future use of upper `vstart` bits is to store imprecise trap information.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: The `vstart` CSR is writable by unprivileged code, but non-zero `vstart` values may cause vector instructions to run substantially slower on some implementations, so `vstart` should not be used by application programmers. A few vector instructions cannot be executed with a non-zero `vstart` value and will raise an illegal-instruction exception as defined below.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: Implementations are permitted to raise illegal-instruction exceptions when attempting to execute a vector instruction with a value of `vstart` that the implementation can never produce when executing that same instruction with the same `vtype` setting.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: Such implementations are permitted to raise an illegal-instruction exception when attempting to execute a vector arithmetic instruction when `vstart` is nonzero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: When migrating a software thread between two harts with different microarchitectures, the `vstart` value might not be supported by the new hart microarchitecture.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: The runtime on the receiving hart might then have to emulate instruction execution up to the next supported `vstart` element position.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: Alternatively, migration events can be constrained to only occur at mutually supported `vstart` locations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Start Index (`vstart`) Register

chunks.text: The upper bits, `vxrm[XLEN-1:2]`, should be written as zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: The vector fixed-point rounding-mode is given a separate CSR address to allow independent access, but is also reflected as a field in `vcsr`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: The fixed-point rounding algorithm is specified as follows.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: Suppose the pre-rounding result is `v`, and `d` bits of that result are to be rounded off.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: Then the rounded result is `(v >> d) + r`, where `r` depends on the rounding mode as specified in the following table.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: The rounding functions: ---- roundoffunsigned(v, d) = (unsigned(v) >> d) + r roundoffsigned(v, d) = (signed(v) >> d) + r ---- are used to represent this operation in the instruction descriptions below.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Rounding Mode (`vxrm`) Register

chunks.text: The `vxsat` CSR has a single read-write least-significant bit (`vxsat[0]`) that indicates if a fixed-point instruction has had to saturate an output value to fit into a destination format.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Saturation Flag (`vxsat`)

chunks.text: Bits `vxsat[XLEN-1:1]` should be written as zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Saturation Flag (`vxsat`)

chunks.text: The `vxsat` bit is mirrored in `vcsr`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Fixed-Point Saturation Flag (`vxsat`)

chunks.text: The `vxrm` and `vxsat` separate CSRs can also be accessed via fields in the XLEN-bit-wide vector control and status CSR, `vcsr`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Control and Status (`vcsr`) Register

chunks.text: | XLEN-1:3 | | Reserved | 2:1 | vxrm[1:0] | Fixed-point rounding mode | 0 | vxsat | Fixed-point accrued saturation flag |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > Vector Control and Status (`vcsr`) Register

chunks.text: The vector extension must have a consistent state at reset.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > State of Vector Extension at Reset

chunks.text: In particular, `vtype` and `vl` must have values that can be read and then restored with a single `vsetvl` instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > State of Vector Extension at Reset

chunks.text: The `vstart`, `vxrm`, `vxsat` CSRs can have arbitrary values at reset.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > State of Vector Extension at Reset

chunks.text: The `vxrm` and `vxsat` fields should be reset explicitly in software before use.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Extension Programmer's Model > State of Vector Extension at Reset

chunks.text: The following diagrams illustrate how different width elements are packed into the bytes of a vector register depending on the current SEW and LMUL settings, as well as implementation VLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State

chunks.text: Elements are packed into each vector register with the least-significant byte in the lowest-numbered bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State

chunks.text: The mapping was chosen to provide the simplest and most portable model for software, but might appear to incur large wiring cost for wider vector datapaths on certain operations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State

chunks.text: When LMUL=1, elements are simply packed in order from the least-significant to most-significant bits of the vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1

chunks.text: Bits within an element are numbered in a little-endian format with increasing bit index from right to left corresponding to increasing magnitude.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1

chunks.text: The element index is given in hexadecimal and is shown placed at the least-significant byte of the stored element.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1

chunks.text: SEW=8b 3 2 1 0 SEW=16b 1 0 SEW=32b 0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1

chunks.text: Byte F E D C B A 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL = 1

chunks.text: When LMUL < 1, only the first LMUL*VLEN/SEW elements in the vector register are used.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL < 1

chunks.text: The remaining space in the vector register is treated as part of the tail, and hence must obey the vta setting.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL < 1

chunks.text: Byte F E D C B A 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL < 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL < 1

chunks.text: When vector registers are grouped, the elements of the vector register group are packed contiguously in element order beginning with the lowest-numbered vector register and moving to the next-highest-numbered vector register in the group once each vector register is filled.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping for LMUL > 1

chunks.text: The recommended software strategy when operating on multiple vectors with different precision values is to modify `vtype` dynamically to keep SEW/LMUL constant (and hence VLMAX constant).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: The following example shows four different packed element widths (8b, 16b, 32b, 64b) in a VLEN=128b implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: The vector register grouping factor (LMUL) is increased by the relative element size such that each group can hold the same number of vector elements (VLMAX=8 in this example) to simplify strip-mining code.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: vn 7 6 5 4 3 2 1 0 SEW=16b, LMUL=1
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: The following table shows each possible constant SEW/LMUL operating point for loops with mixed-width operations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: Each column represents a constant SEW/LMUL operating point.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: Entries in table are the LMUL values that yield that column's SEW/LMUL value for the data width on that row.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: In each column, an LMUL setting for a data width indicates that it can be aligned with the other data widths in the same column that also have an LMUL setting, such that all have the same VLMAX.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: Larger LMUL settings can also used to simply increase vector length to reduce instruction fetch and dispatch overheads in cases where fewer vector register groups are needed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mapping across Mixed-Width Operations

chunks.text: A vector mask occupies only one vector register regardless of SEW and LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mask Register Layout

chunks.text: Each element is allocated a single mask bit in a mask vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mask Register Layout

chunks.text: The mask bit for element i is located in bit i of the mask register, independent of SEW or LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Mapping of Vector Elements to Vector Register State > Mask Register Layout

chunks.text: Vector loads and stores are encoded within the scalar floating-point load and store major opcodes (LOAD-FP/STORE-FP).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats

chunks.text: By default, for most operands of most instructions, EEW=SEW and EMUL=LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Operands

chunks.text: Vector operands or results may occupy one or more vector registers depending on EMUL, but are always specified using the lowest-numbered vector register in the group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Operands

chunks.text: An encoding that would result in the same vector register being read with two or more different EEWs, including when the vector register appears at different positions within two or more vector register groups, is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Operands

chunks.text: A destination vector register group can overlap a source vector register group only if one of the following holds:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Operands

chunks.text: When source and destination registers overlap and have different EEW, the instruction is mask- and tail-agnostic, regardless of the setting of the `vta` and `vma` bits in `vtype`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Operands

chunks.text: Element operations that are masked off (inactive) never generate exceptions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Masking

chunks.text: The destination vector register elements corresponding to masked-off elements are handled with either a mask-undisturbed or mask-agnostic policy depending on the setting of the `vma` bit in `vtype` ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Masking

chunks.text: Where available, masking is encoded in a single-bit `vm` field in the instruction (`inst[25]`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Masking > Mask Encoding

chunks.text: Vector masking is represented in assembler code as another vector operand, with `.t` indicating that the operation occurs when `v0.mask[i]` is `1` (`t` for "true").
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Masking > Mask Encoding

chunks.text: If no masking operand is specified, unmasked vector execution (`vm=1`) is assumed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Vector Masking > Mask Encoding

chunks.text: The prestart elements are those whose element index is less than the initial value in the `vstart` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The prestart elements do not raise exceptions and do not update the destination vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The body elements are those whose element index is greater than or equal to the initial value in the `vstart` register, and less than the current vector length setting in `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The active elements can raise exceptions and update the destination vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The inactive elements do not raise exceptions and do not update any destination vector register group unless masked agnostic is specified (`vtype.vma`=1), in which case inactive elements may be overwritten with 1s.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The tail elements during a vector instruction's execution are the elements past the current vector length setting specified in `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The tail elements do not raise exceptions, and do not update any destination vector register group unless tail agnostic is specified (`vtype.vta`=1), in which case tail elements may be overwritten with 1s, or with the result of the instruction in the case of mask-producing instructions except for mask loads.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: When LMUL < 1, the tail includes the elements past VLMAX that are held in the same vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: ---- for element index x prestart(x) = (0 <= x < vstart) body(x) = (vstart <= x < vl) tail(x) = (vl <= x < max(VLMAX,VLEN/SEW)) mask(x) = unmasked || v0.mask[x] 1 active(x) = body(x) && mask(x) inactive(x) = body(x) && !mask(x) ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: When `vstart` {ge} `vl`, there are no body elements, and no elements are updated in any destination vector register group, including that no tail elements are updated with agnostic values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: Instructions that write an `x` register or `f` register do so even when `vstart` {ge} `vl`, including when `vl`=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The general policy is to return the value 0 when the index is greater than VLMAX in the source vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Instruction Formats > Prestart, Active, Inactive, Body, and Tail Element Definitions

chunks.text: The application specifies the total number of elements to be processed (the application vector length or AVL) as a candidate value for `vl`, and the hardware responds via a general-purpose register with the (frequently smaller) number of elements that the hardware will handle per iteration (stored in `vl`), based on the microarchitectural implementation and the `vtype` setting. A straightforward loop structure, shown in , depicts the ease with which the code keeps track of the remaining number of elements and the amount per iteration handled by hardware.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`)

chunks.text: A set of instructions is provided to allow rapid configuration of the values in `vl` and `vtype` to match application needs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`)

chunks.text: The `vset{i}vl{i}` instructions set the `vtype` and `vl` CSRs based on their arguments, and write the new value of `vl` into `rd`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`)

chunks.text: ---- vsetvli rd, rs1, vtypei rd = new vl, rs1 = AVL, vtypei = new vtype setting vsetivli rd, uimm, vtypei rd = new vl, uimm = AVL, vtypei = new vtype setting vsetvl rd, rs1, rs2 rd = new vl, rs1 = AVL, rs2 = new vtype value ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`)

chunks.text: The new `vtype` value is encoded in the immediate fields of `vsetvli` and `vsetivli`, and in the `rs2` register for `vsetvl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding

chunks.text: ---- Suggested assembler names used for vset{i}vli vtypei immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding

chunks.text: Examples: vsetvli t0, a0, e8, m1, ta, ma SEW= 8, LMUL=1 vsetvli t0, a0, e8, m2, ta, ma SEW= 8, LMUL=2 vsetvli t0, a0, e32, mf2, ta, ma SEW=32, LMUL=1/2 ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding

chunks.text: The `vsetvl` variant operates similarly to `vsetvli` except that it takes a `vtype` value from `rs2` and can be used for context restore.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding

chunks.text: If the `vtype` value is not supported by the implementation, then the `vill` bit is set in `vtype`, the remaining bits in `vtype` are set to zero, and the `vl` register is also set to zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: However, this would have added the first data-dependent trap on a CSR write to the ISA.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: Implementations could choose to trap when illegal values are written to `vtype` instead of setting `vill`, to allow emulation to support new configurations for forward-compatibility.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: The current scheme supports light-weight runtime interrogation of the supported vector unit configurations by checking if `vill` is clear for a given setting.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: A `vtype` value with `vill` set is treated as an unsupported configuration.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: Implementations must consider all bits of the `vtype` value to determine if the configuration is supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: An unsupported value in any location within the `vtype` value must result in `vill` being set.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: Implementations cannot ignore fields they do not implement.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: All bits must be checked to ensure that new code assuming unsupported vector features in `vtype` traps instead of executing incorrectly on an older implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > `vtype` encoding > Unsupported `vtype` Values

chunks.text: The new vector length setting is based on AVL, which for `vsetvli` and `vsetvl` is encoded in the `rs1` and `rd` fields as follows:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: When rs1 is not `x0`, the AVL is an unsigned integer held in the `x` register specified by rs1, and the new `vl` value is also written to the `x` register specified by rd.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: When rs1=`x0` but rd!=`x0`, the maximum unsigned integer value (`~0`) is used as the AVL, and the resulting VLMAX is written to `vl` and also to the `x` register specified by `rd`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: When rs1=`x0` and rd=`x0`, the instructions operate as if the current vector length in `vl` is used as the AVL, and the resulting value is written to `vl`, but not to a destination register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: This form can only be used when VLMAX and hence `vl` is not actually changed by the new SEW/LMUL ratio.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: Use of the instructions with a new SEW/LMUL ratio that would result in a change of VLMAX is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: Use of the instructions is also reserved if `vill` was 1 beforehand.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: Implementations may set `vill` in either case.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: This design was chosen to ensure `vl` would always hold a legal value for current `vtype` setting.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: The current `vl` value can be read from the `vl` CSR.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: The `vl` value could be reduced by these instructions if the new SEW/LMUL ratio causes VLMAX to shrink, and so this case has been reserved as it is not clear this is a generally useful operation, and implementations can otherwise assume `vl` is not changed by these instructions to optimize their microarchitecture.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: For the `vsetivli` instruction, the AVL is encoded as a 5-bit zero-extended immediate (0--31) in the `rs1` field.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > AVL encoding

chunks.text: The `vset{i}vl{i}` instructions first set VLMAX according to their `vtype` argument, then set `vl` obeying the following constraints:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Constraints on Setting `vl`

chunks.text: Deterministic on any given implementation for same input AVL and VLMAX values .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Constraints on Setting `vl`

chunks.text: These specific properties follow from the prior rules: .. `vl = 0` if `AVL = 0` .. `vl > 0` if `AVL > 0` .. `vl {le} VLMAX` .. `vl {le} AVL` .. a value read from `vl` when used as the AVL argument to `vset{i}vl{i}` results in the same value in `vl`, provided the resultant VLMAX equals the value of VLMAX at the time that `vl` was read
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Constraints on Setting `vl`

chunks.text: Requirement 2 ensures that the first strip-mine iteration of reduction loops uses the largest vector length of all iterations, even in the case of `AVL < 2*VLMAX`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Constraints on Setting `vl`

chunks.text: Requirement 2 also allows an implementation to set vl to VLMAX for `VLMAX < AVL < 2*VLMAX` --
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Constraints on Setting `vl`

chunks.text: The SEW and LMUL settings can be changed dynamically to provide high throughput on mixed-width operations in a single loop. ---- Example: Load 16-bit values, widen multiply to 32b, shift 32b result right by 3, store 32b values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Example of strip mining and changes to SEW

chunks.text: vsetvli x0, x0, e32, m8, ta, ma Operate on 32b values vsrl.vi v8, v8, 3 vse32.v v8, (a2) Store vector of 32b elements slli t1, a3, 2 Multiply elements this iteration by 4 bytes/destination element add a2, a2, t1 Bump pointer sub a0, a0, a3 Decrement count by vl bnez a0, loop Any more? ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Configuration-Setting Instructions (`vsetvli`/`vsetivli`/`vsetvl`) > Example of strip mining and changes to SEW

chunks.text: Vector loads and stores can be masked, and they only access memory or raise exceptions for active elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Masked vector loads do not update inactive elements in the destination vector register group, unless masked agnostic is specified (`vtype.vma`=1).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores

chunks.text: All vector loads and stores may generate and accept a non-zero `vstart` value.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Vector loads and stores are encoded within the scalar floating-point load and store major opcodes (LOAD-FP/STORE-FP).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Instruction Encoding

chunks.text: See | mop[1:0] | specifies memory addressing mode | nf[2:0] | specifies the number of fields in each segment, for segment load/stores | lumop[4:0]/sumop[4:0] | are additional fields encoding variants of unit-stride instructions |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Instruction Encoding

chunks.text: Vector memory unit-stride and constant-stride operations directly encode EEW of the data to be transferred statically in the instruction to reduce the number of `vtype` changes when accessing memory in a mixed-width routine.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Instruction Encoding

chunks.text: The data vector register group has EEW=SEW, EMUL=LMUL, while the offset vector register group has EEW encoded in the instruction and EMUL=(EEW/SEW)*LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: If the vector offset elements are narrower than XLEN, they are zero-extended to XLEN before adding to the base effective address.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: If the vector offset elements are wider than XLEN, the least-significant XLEN bits are used in the address calculation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: If the implementation does not support the EEW of the offset elements, the instruction is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: The vector addressing modes are encoded using the 2-bit `mop[1:0]` field.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: If the accesses are to a strongly ordered IO region, the element accesses can be initiated in any order.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: For implementations with precise vector traps, exceptions on indexed-unordered stores must also be precise.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: Additional unit-stride vector addressing modes are encoded using the 5-bit `lumop` and `sumop` fields in the unit-stride load and store instruction encodings respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | unit-stride load | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | unit-stride, whole register load | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | unit-stride, mask load, EEW=8 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | unit-stride fault-only-first | x | x | x | x | x | other encodings reserved |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: The `nf[2:0]` field encodes the number of fields in each segment.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: Larger values in the `nf` field are used to access multiple contiguous fields within a segment as described below in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: The `nf[2:0]` field also encodes the number of whole vector registers to transfer for the whole vector register load/store instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Addressing Modes

chunks.text: Vector loads and stores have an EEW encoded directly in the instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: If the EMUL would be out of range (EMUL>8 or EMUL<1/8), the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: The vector register groups must have legal register specifiers for the selected EMUL, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: Vector unit-stride and constant-stride use the EEW/EMUL encoded in the instruction for the data values, while vector indexed loads and stores use the EEW/EMUL encoded in the instruction for the index values and the SEW/LMUL encoded in `vtype` for the data values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: Vector loads and stores are encoded using width values that are not claimed by the standard scalar floating-point loads and stores.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: Implementations must provide vector loads and stores with EEWs corresponding to all supported SEW settings.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: | Standard scalar FP | x | 0 | 0 | 1 | 16| FLEN | - | FLH/FSH | Standard scalar FP | x | 0 | 1 | 0 | 32| FLEN | - | FLW/FSW | Standard scalar FP | x | 0 | 1 | 1 | 64| FLEN | - | FLD/FSD | Standard scalar FP | x | 1 | 0 | 0 | 128| FLEN | - | FLQ/FSQ | Vector 8b element | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 8| 8 | - | VLxE8/VSxE8 | Vector 16b element | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 16| 16 | - | VLxE16/VSxE16 | Vector 32b element | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 32| 32 | - | VLxE32/VSxE32 | Vector 64b element | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 64| 64 | - | VLxE64/VSxE64 | Vector 8b index | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | SEW | SEW | 8 | VLxEI8/VSxEI8 | Vector 16b index | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | SEW | SEW | 16 | VLxEI16/VSxEI16 | Vector 32b index | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | SEW | SEW | 32 | VLxEI32/VSxEI32 | Vector 64b index | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | SEW | SEW | 64 | VLxEI64/VSxEI64 | Reserved | 1 | X | X | X | - | - | - | |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Width Encoding

chunks.text: ---- Vector unit-stride mask load vlm.v vd, (rs1) Load byte vector of length ceil(vl/8)
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Unit-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: Vector unit-stride mask store vsm.v vs3, (rs1) Store byte vector of length ceil(vl/8) ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Unit-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: `vlm.v` and `vsm.v` are encoded with the same `width[2:0]`=0 encoding as `vle8.v` and `vse8.v`, but are distinguished by different `lumop` and `sumop` encodings.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Unit-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: Since `vlm.v` and `vsm.v` operate as byte loads and stores, `vstart` is in units of bytes for these instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Unit-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: However, these instructions also provide a convenient mechanism to use packed bit vectors in memory as mask values, and also reduce the cost of mask spill/fill by reducing need to change `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Unit-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: vd destination, rs1 base address, rs2 byte constant-stride vlse8.v vd, (rs1), rs2, vm 8-bit constant-stride load vlse16.v vd, (rs1), rs2, vm 16-bit constant-stride load vlse32.v vd, (rs1), rs2, vm 32-bit constant-stride load vlse64.v vd, (rs1), rs2, vm 64-bit constant-stride load
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Constant-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: vs3 store data, rs1 base address, rs2 byte constant-stride vsse8.v vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm 8-bit constant-stride store vsse16.v vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm 16-bit constant-stride store vsse32.v vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm 32-bit constant-stride store vsse64.v vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm 64-bit constant-stride store ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Constant-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: When `rs2`=`x0`, then an implementation is allowed, but not required, to perform fewer memory operations than the number of active elements, and may perform different numbers of memory operations across different dynamic executions of the same static instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Constant-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: When `rs2!=x0` and the value of `x[rs2]=0`, the implementation must perform one memory access for each active element (but these accesses will not be ordered).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Constant-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: When repeating ordered vector accesses to the same memory address are required, then an ordered indexed operation can be used.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Constant-Stride Instructions

chunks.text: Vector indexed-unordered load instructions vd destination, rs1 base address, vs2 byte offsets vluxei8.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 8-bit indexed load of SEW data vluxei16.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 16-bit indexed load of SEW data vluxei32.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 32-bit indexed load of SEW data vluxei64.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 64-bit indexed load of SEW data
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Indexed Instructions

chunks.text: Vector indexed-ordered load instructions vd destination, rs1 base address, vs2 byte offsets vloxei8.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 8-bit indexed load of SEW data vloxei16.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 16-bit indexed load of SEW data vloxei32.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 32-bit indexed load of SEW data vloxei64.v vd, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 64-bit indexed load of SEW data
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Indexed Instructions

chunks.text: Vector indexed-unordered store instructions vs3 store data, rs1 base address, vs2 byte offsets vsuxei8.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 8-bit indexed store of SEW data vsuxei16.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 16-bit indexed store of SEW data vsuxei32.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 32-bit indexed store of SEW data vsuxei64.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm unordered 64-bit indexed store of SEW data
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Indexed Instructions

chunks.text: Vector indexed-ordered store instructions vs3 store data, rs1 base address, vs2 byte offsets vsoxei8.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 8-bit indexed store of SEW data vsoxei16.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 16-bit indexed store of SEW data vsoxei32.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 32-bit indexed store of SEW data vsoxei64.v vs3, (rs1), vs2, vm ordered 64-bit indexed store of SEW data ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Indexed Instructions

chunks.text: The unit-stride fault-only-first load instructions are used to vectorize loops with data-dependent exit conditions ("while" loops).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: These instructions execute as a regular load except that they will only take a trap caused by a synchronous exception on element 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: If element 0 raises an exception, `vl` is not modified, and the trap is taken.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: If an element > 0 raises an exception, the corresponding trap is not taken, and the vector length `vl` is reduced to the index of the element that would have raised an exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: Load instructions may overwrite active destination vector register group elements past the element index at which the trap is reported.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: Similarly, fault-only-first load instructions may update active destination elements past the element that causes trimming of the vector length (but not past the original vector length).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: Non-idempotent memory locations can only be accessed when it is known the corresponding element load operation will not be restarted due to a trap or vector-length trimming.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: ---- strlen example using unit-stride fault-only-first instruction
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: The unit-stride versions only allow probing a region immediately contiguous to a known region, and so reduce the security impact when used in unprivileged code.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: Constant-stride and scatter/gather fault-only-first instructions are not provided due to lack of encoding space, but they can also represent a larger security hole, allowing even unprivileged software to easily check multiple random pages for accessibility without experiencing a trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: This standard does not address possible security mitigations for fault-only-first instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: Even when an exception is not raised, implementations are permitted to process fewer than `vl` elements and reduce `vl` accordingly, but if `vstart`=0 and `vl`>0, then at least one element must be processed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: When the fault-only-first instruction takes a trap due to an interrupt, implementations should not reduce `vl` and should instead set a `vstart` value.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: When the fault-only-first instruction would trigger a debug data-watchpoint trap on an element after the first, implementations should not reduce `vl` but instead should trigger the debug trap as otherwise the event might be lost.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Unit-stride Fault-Only-First Loads

chunks.text: The vector load/store segment instructions move multiple contiguous fields in memory to and from consecutively numbered vector registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: The three-bit `nf` field in the vector instruction encoding is an unsigned integer that contains one less than the number of fields per segment, NFIELDS.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: The EMUL setting must be such that EMUL * NFIELDS {le} 8, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: Each field will be held in successively numbered vector register groups.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions
