chunks.text: When EMUL>1, each field will occupy a vector register group held in multiple successively numbered vector registers, and the vector register group for each field must follow the usual vector register alignment constraints (e.g., when EMUL=2 and NFIELDS=4, each field's vector register group must start at an even vector register, but does not have to start at a multiple of 8 vector register number).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: If the vector register numbers accessed by the segment load or store would increment past 31, then the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: The `vl` register gives the number of segments to move, which is equal to the number of elements transferred to each vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: For segment loads and stores, the individual memory accesses used to access fields within each segment are unordered with respect to each other even for ordered indexed segment loads and stores.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: The `vstart` value is in units of whole segments.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: If a trap occurs during access to a segment, it is implementation-defined whether a subset of the faulting segment's accesses are performed before the trap is taken.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions

chunks.text: The assembler prefixes `vlseg`/`vsseg` are used for unit-stride segment loads and stores respectively.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Examples vlseg8e8.v vd, (rs1), vm Load eight vector registers with eight byte fields.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: vsseg3e32.v vs3, (rs1), vm Store packed vector of 3*4-byte segments from vs3,vs3+1,vs3+2 to memory ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: For loads, the `vd` register will hold the first field loaded from the segment.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: For stores, the `vs3` register is read to provide the first field to be stored to each segment.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: ---- Example 1 Memory structure holds packed RGB pixels (24-bit data structure, 8bpp) vsetvli a1, t0, e8, m1, ta, ma vlseg3e8.v v8, (a0), vm v8 holds the red pixels v9 holds the green pixels v10 holds the blue pixels
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Example 2 Memory structure holds complex values, 32b for real and 32b for imaginary vsetvli a1, t0, e32, m1, ta, ma vlseg2e32.v v8, (a0), vm v8 holds real v9 holds imaginary ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: There are also fault-only-first versions of the unit-stride instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: For fault-only-first segment loads, if an exception is detected partway through accessing the zeroth segment, the trap is taken.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: If an exception is detected partway through accessing a subsequent segment, `vl` is reduced to the index of that segment.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: These instructions may overwrite destination vector register group elements past the point at which a trap is reported or past the point at which vector length is trimmed.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Unit-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Examples vsetvli a1, t0, e8, m1, ta, ma vlsseg3e8.v v4, (x5), x6 Load bytes at addresses x5+i*x6 into v4[i], and bytes at addresses x5+i*x6+1 into v5[i], and bytes at addresses x5+i*x6+2 into v6[i].
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Constant-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Examples vsetvli a1, t0, e32, m1, ta, ma vssseg2e32.v v2, (x5), x6 Store words from v2[i] to address x5+i*x6 and words from v3[i] to address x5+i*x6+4 ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Constant-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Accesses to the fields within each segment can occur in any order, including the case where the byte stride is such that segments overlap in memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Constant-Stride Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Vector indexed segment loads and stores move contiguous segments where each segment is located at an address given by adding the scalar base address in the `rs1` field to byte offsets in vector register `vs2`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: However, even for the ordered form, accesses to the fields within an individual segment are not ordered with respect to each other.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: The data vector register group has EEW=SEW, EMUL=LMUL, while the index vector register group has EEW encoded in the instruction with EMUL=(EEW/SEW)*LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: The EMUL * NFIELDS {le} 8 constraint applies to the data vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Examples vsetvli a1, t0, e8, m1, ta, ma vluxseg3ei8.v v4, (x5), v3 Load bytes at addresses x5+v3[i] into v4[i], and bytes at addresses x5+v3[i]+1 into v5[i], and bytes at addresses x5+v3[i]+2 into v6[i].
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Examples vsetvli a1, t0, e32, m1, ta, ma vsuxseg2ei32.v v2, (x5), v5 Store words from v2[i] to address x5+v5[i] and words from v3[i] to address x5+v5[i]+4 ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Segment Instructions > Vector Indexed Segment Loads and Stores

chunks.text: Format for Vector Load Whole Register Instructions under LOAD-FP major opcode
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Format for Vector Store Whole Register Instructions under STORE-FP major opcode
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: These instructions load and store whole vector register groups.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Examples include compiler register spills, vector function calls where values are passed in vector registers, interrupt handlers, and OS context switches.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Software can determine the number of bytes transferred by reading the `vlenb` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The load instructions have an EEW encoded in the `mew` and `width` fields following the pattern of regular unit-stride loads.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Hence, it would have sufficed to provide only EEW=8 variants.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The full set of EEW variants is provided so that the encoded EEW can be used as a hint to indicate the destination register group will next be accessed with this EEW, which aids implementations that rearrange data internally.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The vector whole register store instructions are encoded similar to unmasked unit-stride store of elements with EEW=8.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The `nf` field encodes how many vector registers to load and store using the NFIELDS encoding (Figure ).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The encoded number of registers must be a power of 2 and the vector register numbers must be aligned as with a vector register group, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved. NFIELDS indicates the number of vector registers to transfer, numbered successively after the base.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Only NFIELDS values of 1, 2, 4, 8 are supported, with other values reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: When multiple registers are transferred, the lowest-numbered vector register is held in the lowest-numbered memory addresses and successive vector register numbers are placed contiguously in memory.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The instructions operate with an effective vector length, `evl`=NFIELDS*VLEN/EEW, regardless of current settings in `vtype` and `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The usual property that no elements are written if `vstart` {ge} `vl` does not apply to these instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Similarly, the property that the instructions are reserved if `vstart` exceeds the largest element index for the current `vtype` setting does not apply.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Instead, the instructions are reserved if `vstart` {ge} `evl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The instructions operate similarly to unmasked unit-stride load and store instructions, with the base address passed in the scalar `x` register specified by `rs1`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Implementations are allowed to raise a misaligned address exception on whole register loads and stores if the base address is not naturally aligned to the larger of the size of the encoded EEW in bytes (EEW/8) or the implementation's smallest supported SEW size in bytes (SEW~MIN~/8).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Some subset implementations might not support smaller SEW widths, so are allowed to report misaligned exceptions for the smallest supported SEW even if larger than encoded EEW.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Software environments can mandate the minimum alignment requirements to support an ABI.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: ---- Format of whole register load and store instructions. vl1r.v v3, (a0) Pseudoinstruction equal to vl1re8.v
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl1re8.v v3, (a0) Load v3 with VLEN/8 bytes held at address in a0 vl1re16.v v3, (a0) Load v3 with VLEN/16 halfwords held at address in a0 vl1re32.v v3, (a0) Load v3 with VLEN/32 words held at address in a0 vl1re64.v v3, (a0) Load v3 with VLEN/64 doublewords held at address in a0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl2r.v v2, (a0) Pseudoinstruction equal to vl2re8.v
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl2re8.v v2, (a0) Load v2-v3 with 2*VLEN/8 bytes from address in a0 vl2re16.v v2, (a0) Load v2-v3 with 2*VLEN/16 halfwords held at address in a0 vl2re32.v v2, (a0) Load v2-v3 with 2*VLEN/32 words held at address in a0 vl2re64.v v2, (a0) Load v2-v3 with 2*VLEN/64 doublewords held at address in a0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl4r.v v4, (a0) Pseudoinstruction equal to vl4re8.v
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl4re8.v v4, (a0) Load v4-v7 with 4*VLEN/8 bytes from address in a0 vl4re16.v v4, (a0) vl4re32.v v4, (a0) vl4re64.v v4, (a0)
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl8r.v v8, (a0) Pseudoinstruction equal to vl8re8.v
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vl8re8.v v8, (a0) Load v8-v15 with 8*VLEN/8 bytes from address in a0 vl8re16.v v8, (a0) vl8re32.v v8, (a0) vl8re64.v v8, (a0)
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: vs1r.v v3, (a1) Store v3 to address in a1 vs2r.v v2, (a1) Store v2-v3 to address in a1 vs4r.v v4, (a1) Store v4-v7 to address in a1 vs8r.v v8, (a1) Store v8-v15 to address in a1 ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The primary purpose would be to inform the microarchitecture that the data will be used as a mask.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: The same effect can be achieved with the following code sequence, whose cost is at most four instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: Of these, the first could likely be removed as `vl` is often already in a scalar register, and the last might already be present if the following vector instruction needs a new SEW/LMUL.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Loads and Stores > Vector Load/Store Whole Register Instructions

chunks.text: If an element accessed by a vector memory instruction is not naturally aligned to the size of the element, either the element is transferred successfully or an address-misaligned exception is raised on that element.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Memory Alignment Constraints

chunks.text: If the Ztso extension is implemented, vector memory instructions additionally follow RVTSO at the instruction level.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Memory Consistency Model

chunks.text: Instructions affected by the vector length register `vl` have a control dependency on `vl`, rather than a data dependency.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Memory Consistency Model

chunks.text: The three-bit `funct3` field is used to define sub-categories of vector instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats

chunks.text: The `funct3` field encodes the operand type and source locations.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: All vector floating-point operations use the dynamic rounding mode in the `frm` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: Use of the `frm` field when it contains an invalid rounding mode by any vector floating-point instruction--even those that do not depend on the rounding mode, or when `vl`=0, or when `vstart` {ge} `vl`--is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: Implementations can make all vector FP instructions report exceptions when the rounding mode is invalid to simplify control logic.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: For integer operations, the scalar can be a 5-bit immediate, `imm[4:0]`, encoded in the `rs1` field.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: The value is sign-extended to SEW bits, unless otherwise specified.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: If XLEN>SEW, the least-significant SEW bits of the `x` register are used, unless otherwise specified.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: If XLEN<SEW, the value from the `x` register is sign-extended to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: If FLEN > SEW, the value in the `f` registers is checked for a valid NaN-boxed value, in which case the least-significant SEW bits of the `f` register are used, else the canonical NaN value is used.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: Vector instructions where any floating-point vector operand's EEW is not a supported floating-point type width (which includes when FLEN < SEW) are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: When adding a vector extension to the Zfinx/Zdinx/Zhinx extensions, floating-point scalar arguments are taken from the `x` registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: Vector arithmetic instructions are masked under control of the `vm` field.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: This arrangement retains the existing encoding conventions that instructions that read only one scalar register, read it from `rs1`, and that 5-bit immediates are sourced from the `rs1` field.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Vector Arithmetic Instruction encoding

chunks.text: Widening instruction encodings must follow the constraints in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Widening Vector Arithmetic Instructions

chunks.text: The choice here is motivated by the belief the chosen approach will require fewer `vtype` changes.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Narrowing Vector Arithmetic Instructions

chunks.text: Narrowing instruction encodings must follow the constraints in .
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Arithmetic Instruction Formats > Narrowing Vector Arithmetic Instructions

chunks.text: Unless otherwise stated, integer operations wrap around on overflow.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions

chunks.text: The widening add/subtract instructions are provided in both signed and unsigned variants, depending on whether the narrower source operands are first sign- or zero-extended before forming the double-width sum.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Widening Integer Add/Subtract

chunks.text: If the source EEW is not a supported width, or source EMUL would be below the minimum legal LMUL, the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Extension

chunks.text: To avoid having to provide the cross-product of the number of vector load instructions by the number of data types (byte, word, halfword, and also signed/unsigned variants), we instead add explicit extension instructions that can be used if an appropriate widening arithmetic instruction is not available.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Extension

chunks.text: For each operation (add or subtract), two instructions are provided: one to provide the result (SEW width), and the second to generate the carry output (single bit encoded as a mask boolean).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: Due to encoding constraints, the carry input must come from the implicit `v0` register, but carry outputs can be written to any vector register that respects the source/destination overlap restrictions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: These instructions are encoded as masked instructions (`vm=0`), but they operate on and write back all body elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: `vmadc` and `vmsbc` add or subtract the source operands, optionally add the carry-in or subtract the borrow-in if masked (`vm=0`), and write the resulting carry-out or borrow-out back to mask register `vd`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: If unmasked (`vm=1`), there is no carry-in or borrow-in.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: These instructions operate on and write back all body elements, even if masked.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: For `vadc` and `vsbc`, the instruction encoding is reserved if the destination vector register is `v0`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Add-with-Carry / Subtract-with-Borrow Instructions

chunks.text: The data to be shifted is in the vector register group specified by `vs2` and the shift amount value can come from a vector register group `vs1`, a scalar integer register `rs1`, or a zero-extended 5-bit immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Shift Instructions

chunks.text: The narrowing right shifts extract a smaller field from a wider operand and have both zero-extending (`srl`) and sign-extending (`sra`) forms.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Integer Right Shift Instructions

chunks.text: The shift amount can come from a vector register group, or a scalar `x` register, or a zero-extended 5-bit immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Integer Right Shift Instructions

chunks.text: The destination mask vector register may be the same as the source vector mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: ---- Set if equal vmseq.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmseq.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar vmseq.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if not equal vmsne.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmsne.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar vmsne.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if less than, unsigned vmsltu.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmsltu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm Vector-scalar
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if less than, signed vmslt.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmslt.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if less than or equal, unsigned vmsleu.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmsleu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar vmsleu.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm Vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if less than or equal, signed vmsle.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmsle.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar vmsle.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if greater than, unsigned vmsgtu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm Vector-scalar vmsgtu.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm Vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Set if greater than, signed vmsgt.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm Vector-scalar vmsgt.vi vd, vs2, imm, vm Vector-immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: Following two instructions are not provided directly Set if greater than or equal, unsigned vmsgeu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm Vector-scalar Set if greater than or equal, signed vmsge.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm Vector-scalar ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: The `vmsge{u}.vx` operation can be synthesized by reducing the value of `x` by 1 and using the `vmsgt{u}.vx` instruction, when it is known that this will not underflow the representation in `x`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: The vt argument to the pseudoinstruction must name a temporary vector register that is not same as vd and which will be clobbered by the pseudoinstruction ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: ---- (a < b) && (b < c) in two instructions when mask-undisturbed vmslt.vv v0, va, vb All body elements written vmslt.vv v0, vb, vc, v0.t Only update at set mask ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Compare Instructions

chunks.text: ---- Signed multiply, returning low bits of product vmul.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmul.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Integer Multiply Instructions

chunks.text: Signed multiply, returning high bits of product vmulh.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmulh.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Integer Multiply Instructions

chunks.text: Unsigned multiply, returning high bits of product vmulhu.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmulhu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Integer Multiply Instructions

chunks.text: Signed(vs2)-Unsigned multiply, returning high bits of product vmulhsu.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm Vector-vector vmulhsu.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vector-scalar ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Integer Multiply Instructions

chunks.text: The `vmerge` instructions are encoded as masked instructions (`vm=0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Merge Instructions

chunks.text: The second operand is a vector register group specified by `vs1` or a scalar `x` register specified by `rs1` or a 5-bit sign-extended immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Merge Instructions

chunks.text: These instructions are encoded as unmasked instructions (`vm=1`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Move Instructions

chunks.text: The first operand specifier (`vs2`) must contain `v0`, and any other vector register number in `vs2` is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Move Instructions

chunks.text: Implementations that do not internally reorganize data can dynamically elide this instruction (aside from resetting `vstart` to 0).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Integer Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Integer Move Instructions

chunks.text: The averaging add and subtract instructions right shift the result by one bit and round off the result according to the setting in `vxrm`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Averaging Add and Subtract

chunks.text: The signed fractional multiply instruction produces a 2*SEW product of the two SEW inputs, then shifts the result right by SEW-1 bits, rounding these bits according to `vxrm`, then saturates the result to fit into SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Fractional Multiply with Rounding and Saturation

chunks.text: If the result causes saturation, the `vxsat` bit is set.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Fractional Multiply with Rounding and Saturation

chunks.text: ---- Signed saturating and rounding fractional multiply See vxrm description for rounding calculation vsmul.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm vd[i] = clip(roundoffsigned(vs2[i]*vs1[i], SEW-1)) vsmul.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vd[i] = clip(roundoffsigned(vs2[i]*x[rs1], SEW-1)) ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Fractional Multiply with Rounding and Saturation

chunks.text: When multiplying two N-bit signed numbers, the largest magnitude is obtained for -2^N-1^ * -2^N-1^ producing a result +2^2N-2^, which has a single (zero) sign bit when held in 2N bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Fractional Multiply with Rounding and Saturation

chunks.text: All other products have two sign bits in 2N bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Fractional Multiply with Rounding and Saturation

chunks.text: These instructions shift the input value right, and round off the shifted out bits according to `vxrm`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Scaling Shift Instructions

chunks.text: The data to be shifted is in the vector register group specified by `vs2` and the shift amount value can come from a vector register group `vs1`, a scalar integer register `rs1`, or a zero-extended 5-bit immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Single-Width Scaling Shift Instructions

chunks.text: The scaling shift amount value can come from a vector register group `vs1`, a scalar integer register `rs1`, or a zero-extended 5-bit immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: For `vnclipu`/`vnclip`, the rounding mode is specified in the `vxrm` CSR.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: Rounding occurs around the least-significant bit of the destination and before saturation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: For `vnclipu`, the shifted rounded source value is treated as an unsigned integer and saturates if the result would overflow the destination viewed as an unsigned integer.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: For `vnclip`, the shifted rounded source value is treated as a signed integer and saturates if the result would overflow the destination viewed as a signed integer.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: If any destination element is saturated, the `vxsat` bit is set in the `vxsat` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Fixed-Point Arithmetic Instructions > Vector Narrowing Fixed-Point Clip Instructions

chunks.text: If the EEW of a vector floating-point operand does not correspond to a supported IEEE floating-point type, the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: The current set of extensions include support for 32-bit and 64-bit floating-point values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: When 16-bit and 128-bit element widths are added, they will be also be treated as IEEE 754-2008-compatible values.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: Other floating-point formats may be supported in future extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: If the floating-point unit status field `mstatus.FS` is `Off` then any attempt to execute a vector floating-point instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: Any vector floating-point instruction that modifies any floating-point extension state (i.e., floating-point CSRs or `f` registers) must set `mstatus.FS` to `Dirty`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: If the hypervisor extension is implemented and V=1, the `vsstatus.FS` field is additionally in effect for vector floating-point instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: If `vsstatus.FS` or `mstatus.FS` is `Off` then any attempt to execute a vector floating-point instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: Any vector floating-point instruction that modifies any floating-point extension state (i.e., floating-point CSRs or `f` registers) must set both `mstatus.FS` and `vsstatus.FS` to `Dirty`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions

chunks.text: A vector floating-point exception at any active floating-point element sets the standard FP exception flags in the `fflags` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Exception Flags

chunks.text: Inactive elements do not set FP exception flags.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Exception Flags

chunks.text: ---- Floating-point reciprocal square-root estimate to 7 bits. vfrsqrt7.v vd, vs2, vm ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: This is a unary vector-vector instruction that returns an estimate of 1/sqrt(x) accurate to 7 bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: [cols="1,1,1"] | | Input | Output | Exceptions raised
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: For the non-exceptional cases, the low bit of the exponent and the six high bits of significand (after the leading one) are concatenated and used to address the following table.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: The output of the table becomes the seven high bits of the result significand (after the leading one); the remainder of the result significand is zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: Let the normalized input exponent be equal to the input exponent if the input is normal, or 0 minus the number of leading zeros in the significand otherwise.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: If the input is subnormal, the normalized input significand is given by shifting the input significand left by 1 minus the normalized input exponent, discarding the leading 1 bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: The following table gives the seven MSBs of the output significand as a function of the LSB of the normalized input exponent and the six MSBs of the normalized input significand; the other bits of the output significand are zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Square-Root Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: ---- Floating-point reciprocal estimate to 7 bits. vfrec7.v vd, vs2, vm ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: This is a unary vector-vector instruction that returns an estimate of 1/x accurate to 7 bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: [cols="1,1,1,1"] | | Input (x) | Rounding Mode | Output (y {approx} 1/x) | Exceptions raised
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: Normal inputs with magnitude at least 2^B-1^ produce subnormal outputs; other normal inputs produce normal outputs.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: For the non-exceptional cases, the seven high bits of significand (after the leading one) are used to address the following table.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: The output of the table becomes the seven high bits of the result significand (after the leading one); the remainder of the result significand is zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: Let the normalized input exponent be equal to the input exponent if the input is normal, or 0 minus the number of leading zeros in the significand otherwise.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: If the normalized output exponent is outside the range [-1, 2*B], the result corresponds to one of the exceptional cases in the table above.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: If the input is subnormal, the normalized input significand is given by shifting the input significand left by 1 minus the normalized input exponent, discarding the leading 1 bit.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: The following table gives the seven MSBs of the normalized output significand as a function of the seven MSBs of the normalized input significand; the other bits of the normalized output significand are zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Reciprocal Estimate Instruction

chunks.text: The result takes all bits except the sign bit from the vector `vs2` operands.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Sign-Injection Instructions

chunks.text: The destination mask vector register may be the same as the source vector mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Compare Instructions

chunks.text: When the comparand is a non-NaN constant, the middle two instructions can be omitted.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Compare Instructions

chunks.text: The 10-bit mask produced by this instruction is placed in the least-significant bits of the result elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Classify Instruction

chunks.text: The upper (SEW-10) bits of the result are filled with zeros.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Classify Instruction

chunks.text: A vector-scalar floating-point merge instruction is provided, which operates on all body elements from `vstart` up to the current vector length in `vl` regardless of mask value.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Merge Instruction

chunks.text: The `vfmerge.vfm` instruction is encoded as a masked instruction (`vm=0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Merge Instruction

chunks.text: This instruction is encoded as an unmasked instruction (`vm=1`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Move Instruction

chunks.text: The instruction must have the `vs2` field set to `v0`, with all other values for `vs2` reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Move Instruction

chunks.text: The conversions follow the same rules on exceptional conditions as the scalar conversion instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Single-Width Floating-Point/Integer Type-Convert Instructions

chunks.text: The conversions use the dynamic rounding mode in `frm`, except for the `rtz` variants, which round towards zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Single-Width Floating-Point/Integer Type-Convert Instructions

chunks.text: Results are equivalently rounded and the same exception flags are raised if all but the last halving step use round-towards-odd (`vfncvt.rod.f.f.w`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Narrowing Floating-Point/Integer Type-Convert Instructions

chunks.text: Only the final step should use the desired rounding mode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Floating-Point Instructions > Narrowing Floating-Point/Integer Type-Convert Instructions

chunks.text: The other elements in the destination vector register ( 0 < index < VLEN/SEW) are considered the tail and are managed with the current tail agnostic/undisturbed policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations

chunks.text: For the uncommon case that the source and destination scalar operand are in different vector registers, this instruction will not copy the source into the destination when `vl`=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations

chunks.text: However, it is expected that in most of these cases it will be statically known that `vl` is not zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations

chunks.text: Traps on vector reduction instructions are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations

chunks.text: Vector reduction operations raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations

chunks.text: The `vfredosum` instruction must sum the floating-point values in element order, starting with the scalar in `vs1[0]`--that is, it performs the computation:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Ordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: When the operation is masked (`vm=0`), the masked-off elements do not affect the result or the exception flags.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Ordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: If no elements are active, no additions are performed, so the scalar in `vs1[0]` is simply copied to the destination register, without canonicalizing NaN values and without setting any exception flags.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Ordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: This behavior preserves the handling of NaNs, exceptions, and rounding when auto-vectorizing a scalar summation loop.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Ordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: The implementation must produce a result equivalent to a reduction tree composed of binary operator nodes, with the inputs being elements from the source vector register group (`vs2`) and the source scalar value (`vs1[0]`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: Each operator first computes an exact sum as a RISC-V scalar floating-point addition with infinite exponent range and precision, then converts this exact sum to a floating-point format with range and precision each at least as great as the element floating-point format indicated by SEW, rounding using the currently active floating-point dynamic rounding mode and raising exception flags as necessary. A different floating-point range and precision may be chosen for the result of each operator. A node where one input is derived only from elements masked-off or beyond the active vector length may either treat that input as the additive identity of the appropriate EEW or simply copy the other input to its output.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: The additive identity is +0.0 when rounding down (towards -{inf}) or -0.0 for all other rounding modes.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: The reduction tree structure must be deterministic for a given value in `vtype` and `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: In particular, if no elements are active and the scalar input is NaN, implementations are permitted to canonicalize the NaN and, if the NaN is signaling, set the invalid exception flag.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: Implementations are alternatively permitted to pass through the original NaN and set no exception flags, as with `vfredosum`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Unordered Single-Width Floating-Point Sum Reduction

chunks.text: If no elements are active, the scalar in `vs1[0]` is simply copied to the destination register, without canonicalizing NaN values and without setting any exception flags.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Reduction Instructions > Vector Single-Width Floating-Point Max and Min Reductions

chunks.text: Widening forms of the sum reductions are provided that read and write a double-width reduction result.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Reduction Operations > Vector Widening Floating-Point Reduction Instructions

chunks.text: Vector mask-register logical operations operate on mask registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: Each element in a mask register is a single bit, so these instructions all operate on single vector registers regardless of the setting of the `vlmul` field in `vtype`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: They do not change the value of `vlmul`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: The destination vector register may be the same as either source vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: As with other vector instructions, the elements with indices less than `vstart` are unchanged, and `vstart` is reset to zero after execution.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: Vector mask logical instructions are always unmasked, so there are no inactive elements, and the encodings with `vm=0` are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: Mask elements past `vl`, the tail elements, are always updated with a tail-agnostic policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: ---- vmand.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] && vs1.mask[i] vmnand.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = !(vs2.mask[i] && vs1.mask[i]) vmandn.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] && !vs1.mask[i] vmxor.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] ^^ vs1.mask[i] vmor.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] || vs1.mask[i] vmnor.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = !(vs2.mask[i] || vs1.mask[i]) vmorn.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] || !vs1.mask[i] vmxnor.mm vd, vs2, vs1 vd.mask[i] = !(vs2.mask[i] ^^ vs1.mask[i]) ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: The old `vmandnot` and `vmornot` mnemonics can be retained as assembler aliases for compatibility.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: Several assembler pseudoinstructions are defined as shorthand for common uses of mask logical operations: ---- vmmv.m vd, vs => vmand.mm vd, vs, vs Copy mask register vmclr.m vd => vmxor.mm vd, vd, vd Clear mask register vmset.m vd => vmxnor.mm vd, vd, vd Set mask register vmnot.m vd, vs => vmnand.mm vd, vs, vs Invert bits ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: The `vmcpy.m` assembler pseudoinstruction can be retained for compatibility.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: For implementations that internally rearrange bits according to EEW, a `vmmv.m` instruction with same source and destination can be used as idiom to force an internal reformat into a mask vector.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: The set of eight mask logical instructions can generate any of the 16 possibly binary logical functions of the two input masks:
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: [cols="1,1,1,1,6,6"] | 4+| output | instruction | pseudoinstruction
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | vmxor.mm vd, vd, vd | vmclr.m vd | 1 | 0 | 0 | 0 | vmnor.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | vmandn.mm vd, src2, src1 | | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | vmnand.mm vd, src1, src1 | vmnot.m vd, src1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0 | vmandn.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 1 | 0 | 1 | 0 | vmnand.mm vd, src2, src2 | vmnot.m vd, src2 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 0 | vmxor.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 1 | 1 | 1 | 0 | vmnand.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 0 | 0 | 0 | 1 | vmand.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | vmxnor.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 0 | 1 | 0 | 1 | vmand.mm vd, src2, src2 | vmmv.m vd, src2 | 1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | vmorn.mm vd, src2, src1 | | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | vmand.mm vd, src1, src1 | vmmv.m vd, src1 | 1 | 0 | 1 | 1 | vmorn.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 0 | 1 | 1 | 1 | vmor.mm vd, src1, src2 | | 1 | 1 | 1 | 1 | vmxnor.mm vd, vd, vd | vmset.m vd |
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Mask-Register Logical Instructions

chunks.text: The `vcpop.m` instruction writes `x[rd]` even if `vl`=0 (with the value 0, since no mask elements are active).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector count population in mask `vcpop.m`

chunks.text: Traps on `vcpop.m` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector count population in mask `vcpop.m`

chunks.text: The `vcpop.m` instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector count population in mask `vcpop.m`

chunks.text: If no active element has the value 1, -1 is written to the GPR.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vfirst` find-first-set mask bit

chunks.text: The `vfirst.m` instruction writes `x[rd]` even if `vl`=0 (with the value -1, since no mask elements are active).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vfirst` find-first-set mask bit

chunks.text: Traps on `vfirst` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vfirst` find-first-set mask bit

chunks.text: The `vfirst` instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vfirst` find-first-set mask bit

chunks.text: If there is no set bit in the active elements of the source vector, then all active elements in the destination are written with a 1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsbf.m` set-before-first mask bit

chunks.text: Traps on `vmsbf.m` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsbf.m` set-before-first mask bit

chunks.text: The `vmsbf` instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsbf.m` set-before-first mask bit

chunks.text: The destination register cannot overlap the source register and, if masked, cannot overlap the mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsbf.m` set-before-first mask bit

chunks.text: Traps on `vmsif.m` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsif.m` set-including-first mask bit

chunks.text: The `vmsif` instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsif.m` set-including-first mask bit

chunks.text: The destination register cannot overlap the source register and, if masked, cannot overlap the mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsif.m` set-including-first mask bit

chunks.text: The vector mask set-only-first instruction is similar to set-before-first, except it only sets the first element with a bit set, if any.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsof.m` set-only-first mask bit

chunks.text: Traps on `vmsof.m` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsof.m` set-only-first mask bit

chunks.text: The `vmsof` instruction will raise an illegal-instruction exception if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsof.m` set-only-first mask bit

chunks.text: The destination register cannot overlap the source register and, if masked, cannot overlap the mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > `vmsof.m` set-only-first mask bit

chunks.text: 1 1 1 0 1 0 1 1 v0 contents 1 0 0 1 0 0 0 1 v2 contents 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 v4 contents viota.m v4, v2, v0.t Masked, vtype.vma=0 1 1 1 5 1 7 1 0 v4 results ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: The result value is zero-extended to fill the destination element if SEW is wider than the result.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: If the result value would overflow the destination SEW, the least-significant SEW bits are retained.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: Traps on `viota.m` are always reported with a `vstart` of 0, and execution is always restarted from the beginning when resuming after a trap handler.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: An illegal-instruction exception is raised if `vstart` is non-zero.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: The destination register group cannot overlap the source register and, if masked, cannot overlap the mask register (`v0`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: ---- Compact non-zero elements from input memory array to output memory array sizet compactnonzero(sizet n, const int* in, int* out) { sizet i; int *p = out; for (i=0; i<n; i++) { const int v = *in++; if (v != 0) *p++ = v; } return (sizet) (p - out); } a0 = n a1 = &in a2 = &out
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: compactnonzero: li a6, 0 Clear count of non-zero elements loop: vsetvli a5, a0, e32, m8, ta, ma 32-bit integers vle32.v v8, (a1) Load input vector sub a0, a0, a5 Decrement number done slli a5, a5, 2 Multiply by four bytes vmsne.vi v0, v8, 0 Locate non-zero values add a1, a1, a5 Bump input pointer vcpop.m a5, v0 Count number of elements set in v0 viota.m v16, v0 Get destination offsets of active elements add a6, a6, a5 Accumulate number of elements vsll.vi v16, v16, 2, v0.t Multiply offsets by four bytes slli a5, a5, 2 Multiply number of non-zero elements by four bytes vsuxei32.v v8, (a2), v16, v0.t Scatter using scaled viota results under mask add a2, a2, a5 Bump output pointer bnez a0, loop Any more?
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Iota Instruction

chunks.text: The `vid.v` instruction writes each element's index to the destination vector register group, from 0 to `vl`-1.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Element Index Instruction

chunks.text: The `vs2` field of the instruction must be set to `v0`, otherwise the encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Element Index Instruction

chunks.text: The result value is zero-extended to fill the destination element if SEW is wider than the result.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Element Index Instruction

chunks.text: If the result value would overflow the destination SEW, the least-significant SEW bits are retained.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Mask Instructions > Vector Element Index Instruction

chunks.text: The integer scalar read/write instructions transfer a single value between a scalar `x` register and element 0 of a vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: If SEW > XLEN, the least-significant XLEN bits are transferred and the upper SEW-XLEN bits are ignored.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: If SEW < XLEN, the value is sign-extended to XLEN bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: If SEW < XLEN, the least-significant bits are copied and the upper XLEN-SEW bits are ignored.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: If SEW > XLEN, the value is sign-extended to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: The other elements in the destination vector register ( 0 < index < VLEN/SEW) are treated as tail elements using the current tail agnostic/undisturbed policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Integer Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: The floating-point scalar read/write instructions transfer a single value between a scalar `f` register and element 0 of a vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Floating-Point Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: The other elements in the destination vector register ( 0 < index < VLEN/SEW) are treated as tail elements using the current tail agnostic/undisturbed policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Floating-Point Scalar Move Instructions

chunks.text: Implementations may optimize certain OFFSET values for `vslideup` and `vslidedown`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions

chunks.text: In particular, power-of-2 offsets may operate substantially faster than other offsets.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions

chunks.text: For all of the `vslideup`, `vslidedown`, `v[f]slide1up`, and `v[f]slide1down` instructions, if `vstart` {ge} `vl`, the instruction performs no operation and leaves the destination vector register unchanged.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions

chunks.text: The slide instructions may be masked, with mask element i controlling whether destination element i is written.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions

chunks.text: For `vslideup`, the value in `vl` specifies the maximum number of destination elements that are written.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: The start index (OFFSET) for the destination can be either specified using an unsigned integer in the `x` register specified by `rs1`, or a 5-bit immediate, zero-extended to XLEN bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: If XLEN > SEW, OFFSET is not truncated to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: Destination elements OFFSET through `vl`-1 are written if unmasked and if OFFSET < `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: ---- vslideup behavior for destination elements (vstart < vl)
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: OFFSET is amount to slideup, either from x register or a 5-bit immediate
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: 0 <= i < min(vl, max(vstart, OFFSET)) Unchanged max(vstart, OFFSET) <= i < vl vd[i] = vs2[i-OFFSET] if v0.mask[i] enabled vl <= i < VLMAX Follow tail policy ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-up Instructions

chunks.text: For `vslidedown`, the value in `vl` specifies the maximum number of destination elements that are written.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions

chunks.text: The remaining elements past `vl` are handled according to the current tail policy ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions

chunks.text: The start index (OFFSET) for the source can be either specified using an unsigned integer in the `x` register specified by `rs1`, or a 5-bit immediate, zero-extended to XLEN bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions

chunks.text: If XLEN > SEW, OFFSET is not truncated to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions

chunks.text: ---- vslidedown behavior for source elements for element i in slide (vstart < vl) 0 <= i+OFFSET < VLMAX src[i] = vs2[i+OFFSET] VLMAX <= i+OFFSET src[i] = 0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions
