chunks.text: vslidedown behavior for destination element i in slide (vstart < vl) 0 <= i < vstart Unchanged vstart <= i < vl vd[i] = src[i] if v0.mask[i] enabled vl <= i < VLMAX Follow tail policy ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-down Instructions

chunks.text: Variants of slide are provided that only move by one element but which also allow a scalar integer value to be inserted at the vacated element position.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: The `vslide1up` instruction places the `x` register argument at location 0 of the destination vector register group, provided that element 0 is active, otherwise the destination element update follows the current mask agnostic/undisturbed policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: If XLEN < SEW, the value is sign-extended to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: If XLEN > SEW, the least-significant bits are copied over and the high XLEN-SEW bits are ignored.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: The `vl` register specifies the maximum number of destination vector register elements updated with source values, and remaining elements past `vl` are handled according to the current tail policy ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: ---- vslide1up behavior when vl > 0
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: i < vstart unchanged 0 = i = vstart vd[i] = x[rs1] if v0.mask[i] enabled max(vstart, 1) <= i < vl vd[i] = vs2[i-1] if v0.mask[i] enabled vl <= i < VLMAX Follow tail policy ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-up

chunks.text: The `vl` register specifies the maximum number of destination vector register elements written with source values, and remaining elements past `vl` are handled according to the current tail policy ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: ---- vslide1down.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vd[i] = vs2[i+1], vd[vl-1]=x[rs1] ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: The `vslide1down` instruction places the `x` register argument at location `vl`-1 in the destination vector register, provided that element `vl-1` is active, otherwise the destination element update follows the current mask agnostic/undisturbed policy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: If XLEN < SEW, the value is sign-extended to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: If XLEN > SEW, the least-significant bits are copied over and the high SEW-XLEN bits are ignored.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: i < vstart unchanged vstart <= i < vl-1 vd[i] = vs2[i+1] if v0.mask[i] enabled vstart <= i = vl-1 vd[vl-1] = x[rs1] if v0.mask[i] enabled vl <= i < VLMAX Follow tail policy ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: ---- vfslide1down.vf vd, vs2, rs1, vm vd[i] = vs2[i+1], vd[vl-1]=f[rs1] ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Slide Instructions > Vector Floating-Point Slide-1-down Instruction

chunks.text: The vector register gather instructions read elements from a first source vector register group at locations given by a second source vector register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The index values in the second vector are treated as unsigned integers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The source vector can be read at any index < VLMAX regardless of `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The maximum number of elements to write to the destination register is given by `vl`, and the remaining elements past `vl` are handled according to the current tail policy ().
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The operation can be masked, and the mask undisturbed/agnostic policy is followed for inactive elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: ---- vrgather.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm vd[i] = (vs1[i] >= VLMAX) ? 0 : vs2[vs1[i]]; vrgatherei16.vv vd, vs2, vs1, vm vd[i] = (vs1[i] >= VLMAX) ? 0 : vs2[vs1[i]]; ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The `vrgather.vv` form uses SEW/LMUL for both the data and indices.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The `vrgatherei16.vv` form uses SEW/LMUL for the data in `vs2` but EEW=16 and EMUL = (16/SEW)*LMUL for the indices in `vs1`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: When SEW=8, `vrgather.vv` can only reference vector elements 0-255.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The `vrgatherei16` form can index 64K elements, and can also be used to reduce the register capacity needed to hold indices when SEW > 16.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: If an element index is out of range ( `vs1[i]` {ge} VLMAX ) then zero is returned for the element value.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: Vector-scalar and vector-immediate forms of the register gather are also provided.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: These read one element from the source vector at the given index, and write this value to the active elements of the destination vector register.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The index value in the scalar register and the immediate, zero-extended to XLEN bits, are treated as unsigned integers.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: If XLEN > SEW, the index value is not truncated to SEW bits.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: ---- vrgather.vx vd, vs2, rs1, vm vd[i] = (x[rs1] >= VLMAX) ? 0 : vs2[x[rs1]] vrgather.vi vd, vs2, uimm, vm vd[i] = (uimm >= VLMAX) ? 0 : vs2[uimm] ----
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: For any `vrgather` instruction, the destination vector register group cannot overlap with the source vector register groups, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Register Gather Instructions

chunks.text: The vector mask register specified by `vs1` indicates which of the first `vl` elements of vector register group `vs2` should be extracted and packed into contiguous elements at the beginning of vector register `vd`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction

chunks.text: T`vcompress` is encoded as an unmasked instruction (`vm=1`).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction

chunks.text: A trap on a `vcompress` instruction is always reported with a `vstart` of 0.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction

chunks.text: Executing a `vcompress` instruction with a non-zero `vstart` raises an illegal-instruction exception.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction

chunks.text: This does mean elements in destination register after `vstart` will already have been updated.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Vector Compress Instruction

chunks.text: The `nr` value in the opcode is the number of individual vector registers, NREG, to copy.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: The instructions operate as if EEW=SEW, EMUL = NREG, effective length `evl`= EMUL * VLEN/SEW.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: The usual property that no elements are written if `vstart` {ge} `vl` does not apply to these instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: Similarly, the property that the instructions are reserved if `vstart` exceeds the largest element index for the current `vtype` setting does not apply.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: Instead, the instructions are reserved if `vstart` {ge} `evl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: If `vd` is equal to `vs2`, the instruction does not change any vector register state.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: Implementations that rearrange data internally can treat this instruction as a hint that the register group will next be accessed with an EEW equal to SEW.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: The instruction is encoded as an OPIVI instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: The value of NREG must be 1, 2, 4, or 8, and values of `simm[4:0]` other than 0, 1, 3, and 7 are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: This encoding is chosen as it is close to the related `vmerge` encoding, and it is unlikely the `vsmul` instruction would benefit from an immediate form.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: The source and destination vector register numbers must be aligned appropriately for the vector register group size, and encodings with other vector register numbers are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Permutation Instructions > Whole Vector Register Move

chunks.text: On a trap during a vector instruction (caused by either a synchronous exception or an asynchronous interrupt), the existing `*epc` CSR is written with a pointer to the trapping vector instruction, while the `vstart` CSR contains the element index on which the trap was taken.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling

chunks.text: To ensure forward progress without the `vstart` CSR, implementations would have to guarantee an entire vector instruction can always complete atomically without generating a trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling

chunks.text: We relax the last requirement to allow elements following `vstart` to have been updated at the time the trap is reported, provided that re-executing the instruction from the given `vstart` will correctly overwrite those elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Precise vector traps

chunks.text: In idempotent memory regions, vector store instructions may have updated elements in memory past the element causing a synchronous trap.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Precise vector traps

chunks.text: Non-idempotent memory regions must not have been updated for indices equal to or greater than the element that caused a synchronous trap during a vector store instruction.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Precise vector traps

chunks.text: Except where noted above, vector instructions are allowed to overwrite their inputs, and so in most cases, the vector instruction restart must be from the `vstart` element index.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Precise vector traps

chunks.text: Implementations must ensure forward progress can be eventually guaranteed for the element or segment reported by `vstart`.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Precise vector traps

chunks.text: Imprecise vector traps are traps that are not precise.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Imprecise vector traps

chunks.text: In particular, instructions newer than `*epc` may have committed results, and instructions older than `*epc` may have not completed execution.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Imprecise vector traps

chunks.text: We assume many embedded implementations will generate only imprecise traps for vector instructions on fatal errors, as they will not require resumable traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Imprecise vector traps

chunks.text: Imprecise traps shall report the faulting element in `vstart` for traps caused by synchronous vector exceptions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Imprecise vector traps

chunks.text: There is no support for imprecise traps in the current standard extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Imprecise vector traps

chunks.text: Some profiles may choose to provide a privileged mode bit to select between precise and imprecise vector traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Selectable precise/imprecise traps

chunks.text: Another trap mode can support swappable state in the vector unit, where on a trap, special instructions can save and restore the vector unit microarchitectural state, to allow execution to continue correctly around imprecise traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Exception Handling > Swappable traps

chunks.text: All standard vector extensions have a minimum required VLEN as described below. A set of vector length extensions are provided to increase the minimum vector length of a vector extension.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvl*: Minimum Vector Length Extensions
Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvl*: Minimum Vector Length Extensions

chunks.text: The table lists the minimum VLEN and supported EEWs for each extension as well as what floating-point types are supported.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zve*: Vector Extensions for Embedded Processors

chunks.text: All Zve* extensions have precise traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zve*: Vector Extensions for Embedded Processors

chunks.text: All Zve* extensions support all vector load and store instructions (), except Zve64* extensions do not support EEW=64 for index values when XLEN=32.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zve*: Vector Extensions for Embedded Processors

chunks.text: The Zve32x extension depends on the Zicsr extension.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zve*: Vector Extensions for Embedded Processors

chunks.text: The V vector extension has precise traps.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > V: Vector Extension for Application Processors

chunks.text: The V vector extension depends upon the Zvl128b and Zve64d extensions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > V: Vector Extension for Application Processors

chunks.text: Providing a larger VLEN allows strip-mining code to be elided in some cases for short vectors, but also increases the size of the minimum implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > V: Vector Extension for Application Processors

chunks.text: The V extension supports all vector load and store instructions (), except the V extension does not support EEW=64 for index values when XLEN=32.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > V: Vector Extension for Application Processors

chunks.text: When the Zvfhmin extension is implemented, the `vfwcvt.f.f.v` and `vfncvt.f.f.w` instructions become defined when SEW=16.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvfhmin: Vector Extension for Minimal Half-Precision Floating-Point

chunks.text: When the Zvfh extension is implemented, all instructions in , , , , , and become defined when SEW=16.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvfh: Vector Extension for Half-Precision Floating-Point

chunks.text: Additionally, conversions between 8-bit integers and binary16 values are provided.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvfh: Vector Extension for Half-Precision Floating-Point

chunks.text: The floating-point-to-integer narrowing conversions (`vfncvt[.rtz].x[u].f.w`) and integer-to-floating-point widening conversions (`vfwcvt.f.x[u].v`) become defined when SEW=8.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Standard Vector Extensions > Zvfh: Vector Extension for Half-Precision Floating-Point

chunks.text: As another example, the SHA-256 cryptographic instructions in the Zvknha extension operate on 128-bit values represented as a 4-element group of 32-bit elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups

chunks.text: The element group size (EGS) is the number of elements in one group, and must be a power-of-two (POT).
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Size

chunks.text: Error checking for `vl` is a little more difficult.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Size

chunks.text: Non-POT EGS can also cause large increases in the lowest-common-multiple of element group sizes, which adds constraints to `vl` setting in order to avoid splitting an element group across strip-mine iterations in vector-length-agnostic code.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Size

chunks.text: The element group size is statically encoded in the instruction, often implicitly as part of the opcode.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Size

chunks.text: Vector instructions with EGS > VLMAX are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Size

chunks.text: When an operand is a vector of element groups, the `vl` setting must correspond to an integer multiple of the element group size, with other values of `vl` reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Setting `vl`

chunks.text: When element group instructions are present, an additional constraint is placed on the setting of `vl` based on an AVL value (augmenting ). EGSMAX is the largest EGS supported by the implementation.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Setting `vl`

chunks.text: When AVL > VLMAX, the value of `vl` must be set to either VLMAX or a positive integer multiple of EGSMAX.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Setting `vl`

chunks.text: If EEW is encoded statically in the instruction, or if an instruction has multiple operands containing vectors of element groups with different EEW, an appropriate SEW must be chosen for `vsetvl` instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Setting `vl`

chunks.text: Alternatively, the opcode might encode EEW of all operands statically and ignore the value of SEW when the operation only makes sense for a single size on each operand.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Determining EEW

chunks.text: Each source and destination operand to a vector instruction may have a different element group size, different EMUL, and/or different EEW.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Determining EMUL

chunks.text: It is possible to use LMUL to concatenate multiple vector registers together to support larger EGW>VLEN.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Width

chunks.text: Profiles can set minimum VLEN requirements to inform authors of such software.
chunks.section: Preamble > "V" Extension for Vector Operations, Version 1.0 > Vector Element Groups > Element Group Width

chunks.text: Where possible, we have written this specification to be understandable by all, though we recognize that the motivations and references to algorithms or other specifications and standards may be unfamiliar to those who are not domain experts.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Intended Audience

chunks.text: We hope this aids people's understanding of which aspects of the specification are particularly relevant to them, and which they may (safely!) ignore or pass to a colleague.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Intended Audience

chunks.text: They should understand the motivations for the instructions we include, and be familiar with most of the algorithms and outside standards to which we refer.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Intended Audience

chunks.text: In particular, they should be aware of the literature around efficiently implementing AES and SM4 SBoxes in hardware.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Intended Audience

chunks.text: The standard will not try to anticipate new useful low-level operations which may be useful as building blocks for future cryptographic constructs.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Policies

chunks.text: Regarding side-channel countermeasures: Where relevant, proposed instructions must aim to remove the possibility of any timing side-channels.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Policies

chunks.text: All instructions shall be implemented with data-independent timing.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Policies

chunks.text: That is, the latency of the execution of these instructions shall not vary with different input values.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Policies

chunks.text: Many vector crypto instructions operate on operands that are wider than elements (which are currently limited to 64 bits wide).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Element Groups

chunks.text: As with all vector instructions, the number of elements processed is specified by the vector length `vl`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Element Groups

chunks.text: The number of element groups operated upon is then `vl`/`EGS`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Element Groups

chunks.text: Likewise the starting element group is `vstart`/`EGS`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Element Groups

chunks.text: See for limitations on `vl` and `vstart` for vector crypto instructions.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Element Groups

chunks.text: Instructions that violate the `vl` or `vstart` requirements are reserved.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: LMUL constraints:: For element-group instructions, `LMUL`*`VLEN` must always be at least as large as `EGW`, otherwise an illegal-instruction exception is raised, even if `vl`=0.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: The *Scalar Element Group* operand has `EMUL = ceil(EGW / VLEN)`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: In the case of the `.vs` instructions defined in this specification, `vs2` holds a 128-bit scalar element group.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: For implementations with `VLEN` ≥ 128, `vs2` refers to a single register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: Thus, the `vd` register group must not overlap the `vs2` register.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: However, in implementations where `VLEN` < 128, `vs2` refers to a register group comprised of the number of registers needed to hold the 128-bit scalar element group.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: In this case, the `vd` register group must not overlap this `vs2` register group.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Instruction Constraints

chunks.text: The following contains some guidelines that enable the portability of vector-crypto-based code to implementations with different values for `VLEN`
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: Application Processors:: Application processors are expected to follow the V-extension and will therefore have `VLEN` {ge} 128.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: Implementations with `VLEN` = 128, require that `LMUL` is doubled for these instructions in order to create 256-bit elements across a pair of registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: Therefore, we recommend that libraries that implement SHA-512 and SM3 employ this doubling of `LMUL` to ensure that the software can run on all implementation with `VLEN` {ge} 128.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: While the doubling of `LMUL` for these instructions is safe for implementations with `VLEN` {ge} 256, it may be less optimal as it will result in unnecessary register pressure and might exact a performance penalty in some microarchitectures.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: Therefore, we suggest that in addition to providing portable code for SHA-512 and SM3, libraries should also include more optimal code for these instructions when `VLEN` {ge} 256. //
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: | | Algorithm | Instructions | VLEN | LMUL
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability
Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: On implementations with `VLEN` < 128, these scalar element groups will necessarily be formed across registers.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Software Portability

chunks.text: If `Zve32x` is supported then `Zvkb` or `Zvbb` provide support for EEW of 8, 16, and 32.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview

chunks.text: If `Zve64x` is supported then `Zvkb` or `Zvbb` also add support for EEW 64.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview

chunks.text: All cryptography-specific instructions defined in this Vector Crypto specification (i.e., those in , , , and but not ,, or ) shall be executed with data-independent execution latency as defined in the .
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview

chunks.text: This specification includes a extension that, when implemented, requires certain vector instructions (including , , and ) to be executed with data-independent execution latency.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview

chunks.text: All of these instructions work on 128-bit element groups comprised of four 32-bit elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkg` - Vector GCM/GMAC

chunks.text: GHASH~H~ is defined in the // link:https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-38d/final[NIST Special Publication 800-38D] "Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) and GMAC" cite:[nist:gcm] (NIST Specification).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkg` - Vector GCM/GMAC

chunks.text: To help avoid side-channel timing attacks, these instructions shall be implemented with data-independent timing.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkg` - Vector GCM/GMAC

chunks.text: The number of element groups to be processed is `vl`/`EGS`. `vl` must be set to the number of `SEW=32` elements to be processed and therefore must be a multiple of `EGS=4`. + Likewise, `vstart` must be a multiple of `EGS=4`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkg` - Vector GCM/GMAC

chunks.text: All of these instructions work on 128-bit element groups comprised of four 32-bit elements.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkned` - NIST Suite: Vector AES Block Cipher

chunks.text: On systems with `VLEN`<128, element groups may be formed by concatenating 32-bit elements from two or four registers by using an LMUL =2 and LMUL=4 respectively.

chunks.text: The number of element groups to be processed is `vl`/`EGS`. `vl` must be set to the number of `SEW=32` elements to be processed and therefore must be a multiple of `EGS=4`. + Likewise, `vstart` must be a multiple of `EGS=4`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Extensions Overview > `Zvkned` - NIST Suite: Vector AES Block Cipher

chunks.text: This instruction must always be implemented such that its execution latency does not depend on the data being operated upon.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vaesdf.[vv,vs]

chunks.text: Operation:: [source,sail] -- function clause execute (VAESDF(vs2, vd, suffix)) = { if(LMUL*VLEN < EGW) then { handleillegal(); // illegal-instruction exception RETIREFAIL } else {
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vaesdf.[vv,vs]

chunks.text: foreach (i from egstart to eglen-1) { let keyelem = if suffix "vv" then i else 0; let state : bits(128) = getvelem(vd, EGW=128, i); let rkey : bits(128) = getvelem(vs2, EGW=128, keyelem); let sr : bits(128) = aesshiftrowsinv(state); let sb : bits(128) = aessubbytesinv(sr); let ark : bits(128) = sb ^ rkey; setvelem(vd, EGW=128, i, ark); } RETIRESUCCESS } } --
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vaesdf.[vv,vs]

chunks.text: In the vector-scalar version, `Op1` is the sign-extended or truncated value in scalar register `rs1`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vandn.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: Operation:: [source,sail] -- function clause execute (VANDN(vs2, vs1, vd, suffix)) = { foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { let op1 = match suffix { "vv" => getvelem(vs1, SEW, i), "vx" => sextortruncatetosew(X(vs1)) }; let op2 = getvelem(vs2, SEW, i); setvelem(vd, EEW=SEW, i, ~op1 & op2); } RETIRESUCCESS }
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vandn.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: Each 64-bit element in the `vs2` vector register is carry-less multiplied by either each 64-bit element in `vs1` (vector-vector), or the 64-bit value from integer register `rs1` (vector-scalar).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclmul.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { let op1 : bits (64) = if suffix "vv" then getvelem(vs1,i) else zextortruncatetosew(X(vs1)); let op2 : bits (64) = getvelem(vs2,i); let product : bits (64) = clmul(op1,op2,SEW); setvelem(vd, i, product); } RETIRESUCCESS }
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclmul.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: Each 64-bit element in the `vs2` vector register is carry-less multiplied by either each 64-bit element in `vs1` (vector-vector), or the 64-bit value from integer register `rs1` (vector-scalar).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclmulh.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { let op1 : bits (64) = if suffix "vv" then getvelem(vs1,i) else zextortruncatetosew(X(vs1)); let op2 : bits (64) = getvelem(vs2, i); let product : bits (64) = clmulh(op1, op2, SEW); setvelem(vd, i, product); } RETIRESUCCESS }
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclmulh.[vv,vx]

chunks.text: | Vs2 | input | Input elements | Vd | output | Count of leading zero bits |
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclz.v

chunks.text: foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { let input = getvelem(vs2, SEW, i); for (j = (SEW - 1); j >= 0; j--) if [input[j]] 0b1 then break; setvelem(vd, SEW, i, SEW - 1 - j) } RETIRESUCCESS } --
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vclz.v

chunks.text: | Vs2 | input | Input elements | Vd | output | Count of trailing zero bits |

chunks.text: foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { let input = getvelem(vs2, SEW, i); for (j = 0; j < SEW; j++) if [input[j]] 0b1 then break; setvelem(vd, SEW, i, j) } RETIRESUCCESS } --

chunks.text: This instruction treats all of the inputs and outputs as 128-bit polynomials and performs operations over GF[2].
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vghsh.vv

chunks.text: The multiplication over GF(2^128^) is a carry-less multiply of two 128-bit polynomials modulo GHASH's irreducible polynomial (x^128^ + x^7^ + x^2^ + x + 1).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vghsh.vv

chunks.text: This instruction must always be implemented such that its execution latency does not depend on the data being operated upon.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vghsh.vv

chunks.text: for (int bit = 0; bit < 128; bit++) { if bittobool(S[bit]) Z ^= H
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vghsh.vv

chunks.text: bool reduce = bittobool(H[127]); H = H << 1; // left shift H by 1 if (reduce) H ^= 0x87; // Reduce using x^7 + x^2 + x^1 + 1 polynomial }
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vghsh.vv

chunks.text: This instruction treats all of the inputs and outputs as 128-bit polynomials and performs operations over GF[2].
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vgmul.vv

chunks.text: It produces the product over GF(2^128^) of the two 128-bit inputs.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vgmul.vv

chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vgmul.vv

chunks.text: Operation:: [source,sail] -- function clause execute (VREV8(vs2)) = { foreach (i from vstart to vl-1) { input = getvelem(vs2, SEW, i); let output : SEW = 0; let j = SEW - 1; foreach (k from 0 to (SEW - 8) by 8) { output[k..(k + 7)] = input[(j - 7)..j]; j = j - 8; setvelem(vd, SEW, i, output) } RETIRESUCCESS } --
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vrev8.v

chunks.text: Operation:: [source,sail] -- function clause execute (VROLVV(vs2, vs1, vd)) = { foreach (i from vstart to vl - 1) { setvelem(vd, EEW=SEW, i, getvelem(vs2, i) <<< (getvelem(vs1, i) & (SEW-1)) ) } RETIRESUCCESS }

chunks.text: Two words of `vs1` are processed with the 8 words of current state held in `vd` and `vs2` to perform two rounds of hash computation producing four words of the next state.

chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsha2ms.vv

chunks.text: The current state of eight 32-bit words is read in as an element group from `vd`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: Eight 32-bit message words are read in as an element group from `vs2`, although only four of them are used.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: All of the 32-bit input words are byte-swapped from big endian to little endian.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: These inputs are processed somewhat differently based on the round group (as specified in rnds), and the next state is generated as an element group of eight 32-bit words.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: The next state of eight 32-bit words are generated, swapped from little endian to big endian, and are returned in an eight-element group.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: The round number is provided by the 5-bit `rnds` unsigned immediate.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: Operation:: [source,sail] -- function clause execute (VSM3C(rnds, vs2, vd)) = { if(LMUL*VLEN < EGW) then { handleillegal(); // illegal-instruction exception RETIREFAIL } else {
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3c.vi

chunks.text: The sixteen most recent 32-bit message words are read in as two eight-element groups from `vs1` and `vs2`.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3me.vv

chunks.text: The next eight 32-bit message words are generated, swapped from little endian to big endian, and are returned in an eight-element group.
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vsm3me.vv

chunks.text: The elements in `vs2` are zero-extended to 2*`SEW` bits, then shifted left by the shift amount specified by either the corresponding elements of `vs1` (vector-vector), integer register `rs1` (vector-scalar), or an immediate value (vector-immediate).
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vwsll.[vv,vx,vi]

chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vwsll.[vv,vx,vi]

chunks.text: function clause execute (VWSLLVX(vs2, rs1, vd)) = { foreach (i from vstart to vl - 1) { setvelem(vd, EEW=2*SEW, i, getvelem(vs2, i) << (X(rs1) & ((2*SEW)-1)) ) } RETIRESUCCESS }
chunks.section: Preamble > Cryptography Extensions: Vector Instructions, Version 1.0 > Instructions > vwsll.[vv,vx,vi]

chunks.text: function clause execute (VWSLLVI(vs2, uimm[4:0], vd)) = { foreach (i from vstart to vl - 1) { setvelem(vd, EEW=2*SEW, i, getvelem(vs2, i) << (uimm[4:0] & ((2*SEW)-1)) ) } RETIRESUCCESS } --
